authors_text: Margaret Mitchell,Alexandra Sasha Luccioni,Nathan Lambert,Marissa Gerchick,Angelina McMillan-Major,Ezinwanne Ozoani,Nazneen Rajani,Tristan Thrush,Yacine Jernite,Douwe Kiela
title: Measuring Data
title_zh: 测量数据
abstract: We identify the task of measuring data to quantitatively characterize the composition of machine learning data and datasets. Similar to an object's height, width, and volume, data measurements quantify different attributes of data along common dimensions that support comparison. Several lines of research have proposed what we refer to as measurements, with differing terminology; we bring some of this work together, particularly in fields of computer vision and language, and build from it to motivate measuring data as a critical component of responsible AI development. Measuring data aids in systematically building and analyzing machine learning (ML) data towards specific goals and gaining better control of what modern ML systems will learn. We conclude with a discussion of the many avenues of future work, the limitations of data measurements, and how to leverage these measurement approaches in research and practice.
summary: We identify the task of measuring data to quantitatively characterize the composition of machine learning data and datasets. Similar to an object's height, width, and volume, data measurements quantify different attributes of data along common dimensions that support comparison. Several lines of research have proposed what we refer to as measurements, with differing terminology; we bring some of this work together, particularly in fields of computer vision and language, and build from it to motivate measuring data as a critical component of responsible AI development. Measuring data aids in systematically building and analyzing machine learning (ML) data towards specific goals and gaining better control of what modern ML systems will learn. We conclude with a discussion of the many avenues of future work, the limitations of data measurements, and how to leverage these measurement approaches in research and practice.
abstract_zh: 我们确定测量数据的任务，以定量表征机器学习数据和数据集的组成。与对象的高度、宽度和体积类似，数据测量沿着支持比较的通用维度量化数据的不同属性。几项研究提出了我们所说的测量，但术语各不相同。我们将其中一些工作结合在一起，特别是在计算机视觉和语言领域，并以此为基础来激励测量数据，将其作为负责任的人工智能开发的关键组成部分。测量数据有助于系统地构建和分析机器学习 (ML) 数据以实现特定目标，并更好地控制现代 ML 系统将学习的内容。最后，我们讨论了未来工作的多种途径、数据测量的局限性以及如何在研究和实践中利用这些测量方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种测量数据的方法，通过量化数据集的属性来支持比较，促进负责任的AI开发。
AI.motivation: 现有研究中缺乏统一的数据测量方法，需整合不同领域的成果，以系统化构建和分析ML数据，确保AI开发的可控性。
AI.method: 通过整合计算机视觉和自然语言处理领域中的测量方法，构建统一的数据测量框架，定义常见维度的量化指标。
AI.result: 提出了一种统一的数据测量框架，明确了数据属性的量化维度，并验证了其在机器学习数据系统化分析中的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 数据测量是负责任AI开发的关键，未来需进一步完善测量维度，解决其局限性，并在实际应用中推广这些方法。

conclusion: 数据测量是负责任AI开发的关键，未来需进一步完善测量维度，解决其局限性，并在实际应用中推广这些方法。
search_text: Measuring Data 测量数据 We identify the task of measuring data to quantitatively characterize the composition of machine learning data and datasets. Similar to an object's height, width, and volume, data measurements quantify different attributes of data along common dimensions that support comparison. Several lines of research have proposed what we refer to as measurements, with differing terminology; we bring some of this work together, particularly in fields of computer vision and language, and build from it to motivate measuring data as a critical component of responsible AI development. Measuring data aids in systematically building and analyzing machine learning (ML) data towards specific goals and gaining better control of what modern ML systems will learn. We conclude with a discussion of the many avenues of future work, the limitations of data measurements, and how to leverage these measurement approaches in research and practice. 我们确定测量数据的任务，以定量表征机器学习数据和数据集的组成。与对象的高度、宽度和体积类似，数据测量沿着支持比较的通用维度量化数据的不同属性。几项研究提出了我们所说的测量，但术语各不相同。我们将其中一些工作结合在一起，特别是在计算机视觉和语言领域，并以此为基础来激励测量数据，将其作为负责任的人工智能开发的关键组成部分。测量数据有助于系统地构建和分析机器学习 (ML) 数据以实现特定目标，并更好地控制现代 ML 系统将学习的内容。最后，我们讨论了未来工作的多种途径、数据测量的局限性以及如何在研究和实践中利用这些测量方法。 本文提出了一种测量数据的方法，通过量化数据集的属性来支持比较，促进负责任的AI开发。 Margaret Mitchell,Alexandra Sasha Luccioni,Nathan Lambert,Marissa Gerchick,Angelina McMillan-Major,Ezinwanne Ozoani,Nazneen Rajani,Tristan Thrush,Yacine Jernite,Douwe Kiela cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Meisam Kabiri,Claudio Cimarelli,Hriday Bavle,Jose Luis Sanchez-Lopez,Holger Voos
title: A Review of Radio Frequency Based Localization for Aerial and Ground Robots with 5G Future Perspectives
title_zh: 展望 5G 未来的空中和地面机器人射频定位综述
abstract: Efficient localization plays a vital role in many modern applications of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGV) and Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), which would contribute to improved control, safety, power economy, etc. The ubiquitous 5G NR (New Radio) cellular network will provide new opportunities for enhancing localization of UAVs and UGVs. In this paper, we review the radio frequency (RF) based approaches for localization. We review the RF features that can be utilized for localization and investigate the current methods suitable for Unmanned vehicles under two general categories: range-based and fingerprinting. The existing state-of-the-art literature on RF-based localization for both UAVs and UGVs is examined, and the envisioned 5G NR for localization enhancement, and the future research direction are explored.
summary: Efficient localization plays a vital role in many modern applications of Unmanned Ground Vehicles (UGV) and Unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs), which would contribute to improved control, safety, power economy, etc. The ubiquitous 5G NR (New Radio) cellular network will provide new opportunities for enhancing localization of UAVs and UGVs. In this paper, we review the radio frequency (RF) based approaches for localization. We review the RF features that can be utilized for localization and investigate the current methods suitable for Unmanned vehicles under two general categories: range-based and fingerprinting. The existing state-of-the-art literature on RF-based localization for both UAVs and UGVs is examined, and the envisioned 5G NR for localization enhancement, and the future research direction are explored.
abstract_zh: 高效的本地化在无人地面车辆（UGV）和无人机（UAV）的许多现代应用中发挥着至关重要的作用，这将有助于提高控制、安全性、电力经济性等。无处不在的5G NR（新无线电）蜂窝网络将为增强无人机和无人飞行器的本地化提供新的机遇。在本文中，我们回顾了基于射频（RF）的定位方法。我们回顾了可用于定位的射频功能，并研究了当前适用于无人驾驶车辆的方法，分为两大类：基于距离的和指纹识别。研究了有关无人机和无人地面车辆基于 RF 的定位的现有最新文献，并探讨了用于增强定位的 5G NR 设想，并探讨了未来的研究方向。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了基于射频（RF）的空中和地面机器人定位方法，并探讨了5G技术对未来定位的潜在影响。
AI.motivation: 高效定位对无人地面车辆（UGV）和无人机（UAV）的控制、安全性和能耗至关重要，而5G NR网络为提升定位性能提供了新机遇。
AI.method: 通过文献综述，分析RF特征并分类为基于距离和指纹识别两类方法，同时探讨5G NR在定位增强中的应用前景。
AI.result: 总结了当前UGV和UAV的RF定位技术现状，明确了5G NR在提升定位精度和覆盖范围中的潜力，并指出未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: RF定位技术需结合5G特性进一步优化，未来研究应聚焦于多技术融合与动态环境适应性提升。

authors_text: Yifan Gong,Zheng Zhan,Pu Zhao,Yushu Wu,Chao Wu,Caiwen Ding,Weiwen Jiang,Minghai Qin,Yanzhi Wang
title: All-in-One: A Highly Representative DNN Pruning Framework for Edge Devices with Dynamic Power Management
title_zh: All-in-One：具有动态电源管理功能的边缘设备的高度代表性 DNN 修剪框架
abstract: During the deployment of deep neural networks (DNNs) on edge devices, many research efforts are devoted to the limited hardware resource. However, little attention is paid to the influence of dynamic power management. As edge devices typically only have a budget of energy with batteries (rather than almost unlimited energy support on servers or workstations), their dynamic power management often changes the execution frequency as in the widely-used dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) technique. This leads to highly unstable inference speed performance, especially for computation-intensive DNN models, which can harm user experience and waste hardware resources. We firstly identify this problem and then propose All-in-One, a highly representative pruning framework to work with dynamic power management using DVFS. The framework can use only one set of model weights and soft masks (together with other auxiliary parameters of negligible storage) to represent multiple models of various pruning ratios. By re-configuring the model to the corresponding pruning ratio for a specific execution frequency (and voltage), we are able to achieve stable inference speed, i.e., keeping the difference in speed performance under various execution frequencies as small as possible. Our experiments demonstrate that our method not only achieves high accuracy for multiple models of different pruning ratios, but also reduces their variance of inference latency for various frequencies, with minimal memory consumption of only one model and one soft mask.
summary: During the deployment of deep neural networks (DNNs) on edge devices, many research efforts are devoted to the limited hardware resource. However, little attention is paid to the influence of dynamic power management. As edge devices typically only have a budget of energy with batteries (rather than almost unlimited energy support on servers or workstations), their dynamic power management often changes the execution frequency as in the widely-used dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) technique. This leads to highly unstable inference speed performance, especially for computation-intensive DNN models, which can harm user experience and waste hardware resources. We firstly identify this problem and then propose All-in-One, a highly representative pruning framework to work with dynamic power management using DVFS. The framework can use only one set of model weights and soft masks (together with other auxiliary parameters of negligible storage) to represent multiple models of various pruning ratios. By re-configuring the model to the corresponding pruning ratio for a specific execution frequency (and voltage), we are able to achieve stable inference speed, i.e., keeping the difference in speed performance under various execution frequencies as small as possible. Our experiments demonstrate that our method not only achieves high accuracy for multiple models of different pruning ratios, but also reduces their variance of inference latency for various frequencies, with minimal memory consumption of only one model and one soft mask.
abstract_zh: 在边缘设备上部署深度神经网络（DNN）期间，许多研究工作都致力于有限的硬件资源。然而，很少有人关注动态电源管理的影响。由于边缘设备通常只有电池的能量预算（而不是服务器或工作站上几乎无限的能量支持），因此它们的动态电源管理通常会改变执行频率，就像广泛使用的动态电压和频率缩放（DVFS）技术一样。这导致推理速度性能高度不稳定，尤其是对于计算密集型的 DNN 模型，这会损害用户体验并浪费硬件资源。我们首先识别这个问题，然后提出 All-in-One，这是一种极具代表性的修剪框架，可使用 DVFS 进行动态电源管理。该框架可以仅使用一组模型权重和软掩模（连同可忽略存储的其他辅助参数）来表示各种剪枝比率的多个模型。通过将模型重新配置为特定执行频率（和电压）对应的剪枝比率，我们能够实现稳定的推理速度，即保持各种执行频率下速度性能的差异尽可能小。我们的实验表明，我们的方法不仅对不同剪枝率的多个模型实现了高精度，而且还减少了不同频率的推理延迟的方差，并且仅一个模型和一个软掩模的最小内存消耗。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为All-in-One的深度神经网络（DNN）剪枝框架，旨在解决边缘设备在动态电源管理下的推理速度不稳定问题。
AI.motivation: 边缘设备依赖电池供电，动态电压和频率调节（DVFS）技术会导致推理速度波动，影响用户体验并浪费硬件资源，现有研究对此关注不足。
AI.method: 通过使用单一模型权重和软掩码（及辅助参数）表示多种不同剪枝比例的模型，根据执行频率动态调整模型配置，以最小化速度差异并保持高精度。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在多种剪枝比例下保持高精度，显著降低不同频率下的推理延迟方差，且仅需存储一个模型和一个软掩码。
AI.conclusion: All-in-One框架有效解决了边缘设备动态电源管理下的推理速度不稳定问题，以极低的内存开销实现了稳定性能。

authors_text: Nick DiSanto,Gavin Harding,Ethan Martinez,Benjamin Sanders
title: Leveraging Contextual Data Augmentation for Generalizable Melanoma Detection
title_zh: 利用上下文数据增强进行通用黑色素瘤检测
abstract: While skin cancer detection has been a valuable deep learning application for years, its evaluation has often neglected the context in which testing images are assessed. Traditional melanoma classifiers assume that their testing environments are comparable to the structured images they are trained on. This paper challenges this notion and argues that mole size, a critical attribute in professional dermatology, can be misleading in automated melanoma detection. While malignant melanomas tend to be larger than benign melanomas, relying solely on size can be unreliable and even harmful when contextual scaling of images is not possible. To address this issue, this implementation proposes a custom model that performs various data augmentation procedures to prevent overfitting to incorrect parameters and simulate real-world usage of melanoma detection applications. Multiple custom models employing different forms of data augmentation are implemented to highlight the most significant features of mole classifiers. These implementations emphasize the importance of considering user unpredictability when deploying such applications. The caution required when manually modifying data is acknowledged, as it can result in data loss and biased conclusions. Additionally, the significance of data augmentation in both the dermatology and deep learning communities is considered.
summary: While skin cancer detection has been a valuable deep learning application for years, its evaluation has often neglected the context in which testing images are assessed. Traditional melanoma classifiers assume that their testing environments are comparable to the structured images they are trained on. This paper challenges this notion and argues that mole size, a critical attribute in professional dermatology, can be misleading in automated melanoma detection. While malignant melanomas tend to be larger than benign melanomas, relying solely on size can be unreliable and even harmful when contextual scaling of images is not possible. To address this issue, this implementation proposes a custom model that performs various data augmentation procedures to prevent overfitting to incorrect parameters and simulate real-world usage of melanoma detection applications. Multiple custom models employing different forms of data augmentation are implemented to highlight the most significant features of mole classifiers. These implementations emphasize the importance of considering user unpredictability when deploying such applications. The caution required when manually modifying data is acknowledged, as it can result in data loss and biased conclusions. Additionally, the significance of data augmentation in both the dermatology and deep learning communities is considered.
abstract_zh: 虽然皮肤癌检测多年来一直是一种有价值的深度学习应用，但其评估往往忽略了评估测试图像的背景。传统的黑色素瘤分类器假设他们的测试环境与他们训练的结构化图像相当。本文挑战了这一观点，并认为痣的大小（专业皮肤病学的一个关键属性）可能会在自动黑色素瘤检测中产生误导。虽然恶性黑色素瘤往往比良性黑色素瘤更大，但当无法进行图像的上下文缩放时，仅依赖大小可能是不可靠的，甚至是有害的。为了解决这个问题，该实现提出了一个自定义模型，该模型执行各种数据增强程序，以防止过度拟合不正确的参数并模拟黑色素瘤检测应用程序的实际使用情况。实现了采用不同形式的数据增强的多个自定义模型，以突出摩尔分类器最重要的特征。这些实现强调了在部署此类应用程序时考虑用户不可预测性的重要性。手动修改数据时需要谨慎，因为这可能会导致数据丢失和有偏见的结论。此外，还考虑了数据增强在皮肤病学和深度学习社区中的重要性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用上下文数据增强技术改进黑色素瘤检测模型的泛化能力，解决传统方法因忽略图像上下文导致的检测偏差问题。
AI.motivation: 现有黑色素瘤检测模型过度依赖痣的大小特征，而实际应用中图像缩放不可控，单纯依赖大小可能导致误判，需考虑真实场景的上下文变化。
AI.method: 设计多组自定义模型，通过模拟真实场景的数据增强策略（如尺度变换、噪声注入等）训练网络，验证不同增强方式对模型鲁棒性的影响。
AI.result: 实验表明数据增强能有效缓解模型对错误特征（如绝对尺寸）的依赖，提升在非结构化图像中的检测准确率，同时凸显用户操作不可预测性带来的挑战。
AI.conclusion: 数据增强是提升医学图像分析模型泛化能力的关键，需在训练阶段主动模拟真实使用场景，避免因数据偏差导致临床误诊风险。

Traditional melanoma classifiers assume that their testing environments are comparable to the structured images they are trained on. This paper challenges this notion and argues that mole size, a critical attribute in professional dermatology, can be misleading in automated melanoma detection. While malignant melanomas tend to be larger than benign melanomas, relying solely on size can be unreliable and even harmful when contextual scaling of images is not possible. To address this issue, this implementation proposes a custom model that performs various data augmentation procedures to prevent overfitting to incorrect parameters and simulate real-world usage of melanoma detection applications. Multiple custom models employing different forms of data augmentation are implemented to highlight the most significant features of mole classifiers. These implementations emphasize the importance of considering user unpredictability when deploying such applications. The caution required when manually modifying data is acknowledged, as it can result in data loss and biased conclusions. authors_text: Neha Sunil,Shaoxiong Wang,Yu She,Edward Adelson,Alberto Rodriguez
title: Visuotactile Affordances for Cloth Manipulation with Local Control
title_zh: 通过局部控制进行布料操作的视觉触觉可供性
abstract: Cloth in the real world is often crumpled, self-occluded, or folded in on itself such that key regions, such as corners, are not directly graspable, making manipulation difficult. We propose a system that leverages visual and tactile perception to unfold the cloth via grasping and sliding on edges. By doing so, the robot is able to grasp two adjacent corners, enabling subsequent manipulation tasks like folding or hanging. As components of this system, we develop tactile perception networks that classify whether an edge is grasped and estimate the pose of the edge. We use the edge classification network to supervise a visuotactile edge grasp affordance network that can grasp edges with a 90% success rate. Once an edge is grasped, we demonstrate that the robot can slide along the cloth to the adjacent corner using tactile pose estimation/control in real time. See http://nehasunil.com/visuotactile/visuotactile.html for videos.
summary: Cloth in the real world is often crumpled, self-occluded, or folded in on itself such that key regions, such as corners, are not directly graspable, making manipulation difficult. We propose a system that leverages visual and tactile perception to unfold the cloth via grasping and sliding on edges. By doing so, the robot is able to grasp two adjacent corners, enabling subsequent manipulation tasks like folding or hanging. As components of this system, we develop tactile perception networks that classify whether an edge is grasped and estimate the pose of the edge. We use the edge classification network to supervise a visuotactile edge grasp affordance network that can grasp edges with a 90% success rate. Once an edge is grasped, we demonstrate that the robot can slide along the cloth to the adjacent corner using tactile pose estimation/control in real time. See http://nehasunil.com/visuotactile/visuotactile.html for videos.
abstract_zh: 现实世界中的布料通常会起皱、自闭塞或自身折叠，使得角落等关键区域无法直接抓握，从而导致操作变得困难。我们提出了一种利用视觉和触觉感知通过抓住和滑动边缘来展开布料的系统。通过这样做，机器人能够抓住两个相邻的角，从而实现后续的操作任务，例如折叠或悬挂。作为该系统的组成部分，我们开发了触觉感知网络，可以对边缘是否被抓住进行分类并估计边缘的姿势。我们使用边缘分类网络来监督视觉触觉边缘抓取可供性网络，该网络可以以 90% 的成功率抓取边缘。一旦抓住边缘，我们就证明机器人可以使用实时触觉姿势估计/控制沿着布料滑动到相邻的角。请参阅 http://nehasunil.com/visuotactile/visuotactile.html 查看视频。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合视觉与触觉感知的系统，通过抓取和滑动布料边缘实现布料展开及后续操作。
AI.motivation: 现实中的布料常因褶皱、遮挡导致关键区域难以直接抓取，传统方法难以有效操作。
AI.method: 开发触觉感知网络分类边缘抓取状态并估计边缘姿态，结合视觉信息训练仿射网络实现边缘抓取，并通过实时触觉控制滑动至相邻角落。
AI.result: 边缘抓取成功率达90%，机器人可稳定滑动至相邻角落完成展开任务。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过多模态感知与局部控制有效解决复杂布料操作难题，为折叠、悬挂等任务提供基础。

authors_text: Zhiqi Kang,Enrico Fini,Moin Nabi,Elisa Ricci,Karteek Alahari
title: A soft nearest-neighbor framework for continual semi-supervised learning
title_zh: 用于持续半监督学习的软最近邻框架
abstract: Despite significant advances, the performance of state-of-the-art continual learning approaches hinges on the unrealistic scenario of fully labeled data. In this paper, we tackle this challenge and propose an approach for continual semi-supervised learning--a setting where not all the data samples are labeled. A primary issue in this scenario is the model forgetting representations of unlabeled data and overfitting the labeled samples. We leverage the power of nearest-neighbor classifiers to nonlinearly partition the feature space and flexibly model the underlying data distribution thanks to its non-parametric nature. This enables the model to learn a strong representation for the current task, and distill relevant information from previous tasks. We perform a thorough experimental evaluation and show that our method outperforms all the existing approaches by large margins, setting a solid state of the art on the continual semi-supervised learning paradigm. For example, on CIFAR-100 we surpass several others even when using at least 30 times less supervision (0.8% vs. 25% of annotations). Finally, our method works well on both low and high resolution images and scales seamlessly to more complex datasets such as ImageNet-100. The code is publicly available on https://github.com/kangzhiq/NNCSL
summary: Despite significant advances, the performance of state-of-the-art continual learning approaches hinges on the unrealistic scenario of fully labeled data. In this paper, we tackle this challenge and propose an approach for continual semi-supervised learning--a setting where not all the data samples are labeled. A primary issue in this scenario is the model forgetting representations of unlabeled data and overfitting the labeled samples. We leverage the power of nearest-neighbor classifiers to nonlinearly partition the feature space and flexibly model the underlying data distribution thanks to its non-parametric nature. This enables the model to learn a strong representation for the current task, and distill relevant information from previous tasks. We perform a thorough experimental evaluation and show that our method outperforms all the existing approaches by large margins, setting a solid state of the art on the continual semi-supervised learning paradigm. For example, on CIFAR-100 we surpass several others even when using at least 30 times less supervision (0.8% vs. 25% of annotations). Finally, our method works well on both low and high resolution images and scales seamlessly to more complex datasets such as ImageNet-100. The code is publicly available on https://github.com/kangzhiq/NNCSL
abstract_zh: 尽管取得了重大进展，但最先进的持续学习方法的性能取决于完全标记数据的不切实际的场景。在本文中，我们解决了这一挑战，并提出了一种持续半监督学习的方法——一种并非所有数据样本都被标记的设置。这种情况下的主要问题是模型忘记了未标记数据的表示并过度拟合了标记样本。我们利用最近邻分类器的强大功能来非线性划分特征空间，并由于其非参数性质而灵活地对底层数据分布进行建模。这使得模型能够学习当前任务的强表示，并从先前的任务中提取相关信息。我们进行了彻底的实验评估，结果表明我们的方法大幅优于所有现有方法，为持续半监督学习范式奠定了坚实的技术基础。例如，在 CIFAR-100 上，即使使用至少 30 倍的监督（注释的 0.8% 与 25%），我们也超越了其他几个。最后，我们的方法在低分辨率和高分辨率图像上都能很好地工作，并且可以无缝缩放到更复杂的数据集，例如 ImageNet-100。该代码可在 https://github.com/kangzhiq/NNCSL 上公开获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于持续半监督学习的软最近邻框架，解决在非全标注数据下模型遗忘和过拟合的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有持续学习方法依赖全标注数据，但实际场景中数据标注成本高且不现实，需开发适用于半监督场景的新方法。
AI.method: 利用最近邻分类器的非参数特性，通过非线性划分特征空间建模数据分布，结合当前任务学习与知识蒸馏技术。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-100数据集上，使用仅0.8%标注数据时性能优于其他方法25%标注数据的水平，并在ImageNet-100等复杂数据集上表现优异。
AI.conclusion: 该方法在持续半监督学习领域达到新SOTA，适用于不同分辨率图像且可扩展至复杂数据集，显著降低标注依赖性。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于持续半监督学习的软最近邻框架，解决在非全标注数据下模型遗忘和过拟合的问题。
ai_content.motivation: 现有持续学习方法依赖全标注数据，但实际场景中数据标注成本高且不现实，需开发适用于半监督场景的新方法。
ai_content.method: 利用最近邻分类器的非参数特性，通过非线性划分特征空间建模数据分布，结合当前任务学习与知识蒸馏技术。
ai_content.result: 在CIFAR-100数据集上，使用仅0.8%标注数据时性能优于其他方法25%标注数据的水平，并在ImageNet-100等复杂数据集上表现优异。
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法在持续半监督学习领域达到新SOTA，适用于不同分辨率图像且可扩展至复杂数据集，显著降低标注依赖性。

search_text: A soft nearest-neighbor framework for continual semi-supervised learning 用于持续半监督学习的软最近邻框架 Despite significant advances, the performance of state-of-the-art continual learning approaches hinges on the unrealistic scenario of fully labeled data. In this paper, we tackle this challenge and propose an approach for continual semi-supervised learning--a setting where not all the data samples are labeled. A primary issue in this scenario is the model forgetting representations of unlabeled data and overfitting the labeled samples. We leverage the power of nearest-neighbor classifiers to nonlinearly partition the feature space and flexibly model the underlying data distribution thanks to its non-parametric nature. This enables the model to learn a strong representation for the current task, and distill relevant information from previous tasks. We perform a thorough experimental evaluation and show that our method outperforms all the existing approaches by large margins, setting a solid state of the art on the continual semi-supervised learning paradigm. For example, on CIFAR-100 we surpass several others even when using at least 30 times less supervision (0.8% vs. 25% of annotations). Finally, our method works well on both low and high resolution images and scales seamlessly to more complex datasets such as ImageNet-100. The code is publicly available on https://github.com/kangzhiq/NNCSL 尽管取得了重大进展，但最先进的持续学习方法的性能取决于完全标记数据的不切实际的场景。在本文中，我们解决了这一挑战，并提出了一种持续半监督学习的方法——一种并非所有数据样本都被标记的设置。这种情况下的主要问题是模型忘记了未标记数据的表示并过度拟合了标记样本。我们利用最近邻分类器的强大功能来非线性划分特征空间，并由于其非参数性质而灵活地对底层数据分布进行建模。这使得模型能够学习当前任务的强表示，并从先前的任务中提取相关信息。我们进行了彻底的实验评估，结果表明我们的方法大幅优于所有现有方法，为持续半监督学习范式奠定了坚实的技术基础。例如，在 CIFAR-100 上，即使使用至少 30 倍的监督（注释的 0.8% 与 25%），我们也超越了其他几个。最后，我们的方法在低分辨率和高分辨率图像上都能很好地工作，并且可以无缝缩放到更复杂的数据集，例如 ImageNet-100。该代码可在 https://github.com/kangzhiq/NNCSL 上公开获取 本文提出了一种用于持续半监督学习的软最近邻框架，解决在非全标注数据下模型遗忘和过拟合的问题。 Zhiqi Kang,Enrico Fini,Moin Nabi,Elisa Ricci,Karteek Alahari cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Robert Clausecker,Daniel Lemire
title: Transcoding Unicode Characters with AVX-512 Instructions
title_zh: 使用 AVX-512 指令转码 Unicode 字符
abstract: Intel includes in its recent processors a powerful set of instructions capable of processing 512-bit registers with a single instruction (AVX-512). Some of these instructions have no equivalent in earlier instruction sets. We leverage these instructions to efficiently transcode strings between the most common formats: UTF-8 and UTF-16. With our novel algorithms, we are often twice as fast as the previous best solutions. For example, we transcode Chinese text from UTF-8 to UTF-16 at more than 5 GiB/s using fewer than 2 CPU instructions per character. To ensure reproducibility, we make our software freely available as an open source library. Our library is part of the popular Node.js JavaScript runtime.
summary: Intel includes in its recent processors a powerful set of instructions capable of processing 512-bit registers with a single instruction (AVX-512). Some of these instructions have no equivalent in earlier instruction sets. We leverage these instructions to efficiently transcode strings between the most common formats: UTF-8 and UTF-16. With our novel algorithms, we are often twice as fast as the previous best solutions. For example, we transcode Chinese text from UTF-8 to UTF-16 at more than 5 GiB/s using fewer than 2 CPU instructions per character. To ensure reproducibility, we make our software freely available as an open source library. Our library is part of the popular Node.js JavaScript runtime.
abstract_zh: Intel 在其最新的处理器中包含了一组强大的指令，能够使用单个指令 (AVX-512) 处理 512 位寄存器。其中一些指令在早期指令集中没有等效指令。我们利用这些指令在最常见的格式之间高效地转码字符串：UTF-8 和 UTF-16。 With our novel algorithms, we are often twice as fast as the previous best solutions.例如，我们使用每个字符不到 2 个 CPU 指令，以超过 5 GiB/s 的速度将中文文本从 UTF-8 转码为 UTF-16。 To ensure reproducibility, we make our software freely available as an open source library.我们的库是流行的 Node.js JavaScript 运行时的一部分。

AI.tldr: 利用AVX-512指令高效实现UTF-8与UTF-16之间的字符串转码
AI.motivation: 利用新型AVX-512指令集的并行计算能力，提升Unicode字符转码效率
AI.method: 设计基于AVX-512指令集的并行算法，优化UTF-8与UTF-16转换过程
AI.result: 实现中文文本转码速度超5GiB/s，每字符消耗少于2条指令，性能较最优解提升2倍
AI.conclusion: 证明AVX-512指令在Unicode转码中的高效性，开源库已集成至Node.js验证实用性

authors_text: Zijin Wang,Ou Zheng,Liangding Li,Mohamed Abdel-Aty,Carolina Cruz-Neira,Zubayer Islam
title: Towards Next Generation of Pedestrian and Connected Vehicle In-the-loop Research: A Digital Twin Co-Simulation Framework
title_zh: 迈向下一代行人和联网车辆的在环研究：数字孪生联合仿真框架
abstract: Digital Twin is an emerging technology that replicates real-world entities into a digital space. It has attracted increasing attention in the transportation field and many researchers are exploring its future applications in the development of Intelligent Transportation System (ITS) technologies. Connected vehicles (CVs) and pedestrians are among the major traffic participants in ITS. However, the usage of Digital Twin in research involving both CV and pedestrian remains largely unexplored. In this study, a Digital Twin framework for CV and pedestrian in-the-loop simulation is proposed. The proposed framework consists of the physical world, the digital world, and data transmission in between. The features for the entities (CV and pedestrian) that need digital twining are divided into external state and internal state, and the attributes in each state are described. We also demonstrate a sample architecture under the proposed Digital Twin framework, which is based on Carla-Sumo Co-simulation and Cave automatic virtual environment (CAVE). A case study that investigates Vehicle-Pedestrian (V2P) warning system is conducted to validate the effectiveness of the presented architecture. The proposed framework is expected to provide guidance to the future Digital Twin research, and the architecture we build can serve as the testbed for further research and development of ITS applications on CV and pedestrians.
summary: Digital Twin is an emerging technology that replicates real-world entities into a digital space. It has attracted increasing attention in the transportation field and many researchers are exploring its future applications in the development of Intelligent Transportation System (ITS) technologies. Connected vehicles (CVs) and pedestrians are among the major traffic participants in ITS. However, the usage of Digital Twin in research involving both CV and pedestrian remains largely unexplored. In this study, a Digital Twin framework for CV and pedestrian in-the-loop simulation is proposed. The proposed framework consists of the physical world, the digital world, and data transmission in between. The features for the entities (CV and pedestrian) that need digital twining are divided into external state and internal state, and the attributes in each state are described. We also demonstrate a sample architecture under the proposed Digital Twin framework, which is based on Carla-Sumo Co-simulation and Cave automatic virtual environment (CAVE). A case study that investigates Vehicle-Pedestrian (V2P) warning system is conducted to validate the effectiveness of the presented architecture. The proposed framework is expected to provide guidance to the future Digital Twin research, and the architecture we build can serve as the testbed for further research and development of ITS applications on CV and pedestrians.
abstract_zh: 数字孪生是一项新兴技术，可将现实世界的实体复制到数字空间中。它在交通领域引起了越来越多的关注，许多研究人员正在探索其在智能交通系统（ITS）技术开发中的未来应用。联网车辆 (CV) 和行人是 ITS 的主要交通参与者。然而，数字孪生在涉及 CV 和行人的研究中的使用在很大程度上仍未得到探索。在本研究中，提出了用于 CV 和行人在环仿真的数字孪生框架。所提出的框架由物理世界、数字世界以及两者之间的数据传输组成。将需要数字孪生的实体（CV和行人）的特征分为外部状态和内部状态，并描述每个状态的属性。我们还演示了所提出的数字孪生框架下的示例架构，该框架基于 Carla-Sumo 协同仿真和 Cave 自动虚拟环境（CAVE）。通过研究车辆-行人（V2P）警告系统的案例研究来验证所提出架构的有效性。所提出的框架有望为未来的数字孪生研究提供指导，我们构建的架构可以作为进一步研究和开发 CV 和行人 ITS 应用的测试平台。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于连接车辆和行人环路仿真的数字孪生协同仿真框架。
AI.motivation: 尽管数字孪生技术在交通领域受到关注，但其在连接车辆（CV）和行人协同研究中的应用仍鲜有探索，亟需构建相关框架以推动智能交通系统（ITS）的发展。
AI.method: 框架包含物理世界、数字世界及两者间的数据传输，将实体状态分为外部状态和内部状态，并基于Carla-Sumo协同仿真与CAVE环境构建示例架构。
AI.result: 通过车辆-行人（V2P）预警系统的案例研究，验证了所提架构的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该框架为未来数字孪生研究提供指导，构建的架构可作为ITS应用进一步研发的测试平台。

authors_text: Alicia Fornés,Asma Bensalah,Cristina Carmona-Duarte,Jialuo Chen,Miguel A. Ferrer,Andreas Fischer,Josep Lladós,Cristina Martín,Eloy Opisso,Réjean Plamondon,Anna Scius-Bertrand,Josep Maria Tormos
title: The RPM3D project: 3D Kinematics for Remote Patient Monitoring
title_zh: RPM3D 项目：用于远程患者监护的 3D 运动学
abstract: This project explores the feasibility of remote patient monitoring based on the analysis of 3D movements captured with smartwatches. We base our analysis on the Kinematic Theory of Rapid Human Movement. We have validated our research in a real case scenario for stroke rehabilitation at the Guttmann Institute5 (neurorehabilitation hospital), showing promising results. Our work could have a great impact in remote healthcare applications, improving the medical efficiency and reducing the healthcare costs. Future steps include more clinical validation, developing multi-modal analysis architectures (analysing data from sensors, images, audio, etc.), and exploring the application of our technology to monitor other neurodegenerative diseases.
summary: This project explores the feasibility of remote patient monitoring based on the analysis of 3D movements captured with smartwatches. We base our analysis on the Kinematic Theory of Rapid Human Movement. Our work could have a great impact in remote healthcare applications, improving the medical efficiency and reducing the healthcare costs. Future steps include more clinical validation, developing multi-modal analysis architectures (analysing data from sensors, images, audio, etc.), and exploring the application of our technology to monitor other neurodegenerative diseases.
abstract_zh: 该项目基于对智能手表捕获的 3D 运动的分析，探索远程患者监测的可行性。我们的分析基于人体快速运动的运动学理论。我们在古特曼研究所5（神经康复医院）的中风康复真实案例中验证了我们的研究，显示出有希望的结果。我们的工作可以对远程医疗应用产生巨大影响，提高医疗效率并降低医疗成本。未来的步骤包括更多的临床验证、开发多模式分析架构（分析来自传感器、图像、音频等的数据），以及探索我们的技术在监测其他神经退行性疾病方面的应用。

AI.tldr: 本项目基于智能手表捕捉的3D运动数据，探索远程患者监测的可行性，重点应用于中风康复领域。
AI.motivation: 通过远程监测技术提升医疗效率，降低医疗成本，为神经退行性疾病的康复提供创新解决方案。
AI.method: 采用运动学理论分析智能手表采集的3D运动数据，结合实际临床场景进行验证。
AI.result: 在Guttmann医院的中风康复案例中验证了技术有效性，未来将扩展多模态数据分析和临床应用范围。
AI.conclusion: 该技术有望推动远程医疗发展，后续需进一步临床验证并拓展至其他神经退行性疾病的监测领域。

search_text: The RPM3D project: 3D Kinematics for Remote Patient Monitoring RPM3D 项目：用于远程患者监护的 3D 运动学 This project explores the feasibility of remote patient monitoring based on the analysis of 3D movements captured with smartwatches. We base our analysis on the Kinematic Theory of Rapid Human Movement. We have validated our research in a real case scenario for stroke rehabilitation at the Guttmann Institute5 (neurorehabilitation hospital), showing promising results. Our work could have a great impact in remote healthcare applications, improving the medical efficiency and reducing the healthcare costs. Future steps include more clinical validation, developing multi-modal analysis architectures (analysing data from sensors, images, audio, etc.), and exploring the application of our technology to monitor other neurodegenerative diseases. 该项目基于对智能手表捕获的 3D 运动的分析，探索远程患者监测的可行性。我们的分析基于人体快速运动的运动学理论。我们在古特曼研究所5（神经康复医院）的中风康复真实案例中验证了我们的研究，显示出有希望的结果。我们的工作可以对远程医疗应用产生巨大影响，提高医疗效率并降低医疗成本。未来的步骤包括更多的临床验证、开发多模式分析架构（分析来自传感器、图像、音频等的数据），以及探索我们的技术在监测其他神经退行性疾病方面的应用。 本项目基于智能手表捕捉的3D运动数据，探索远程患者监测的可行性，重点应用于中风康复领域。 Alicia Fornés,Asma Bensalah,Cristina Carmona-Duarte,Jialuo Chen,Miguel A. authors_text: Asma Bensalah,Jialuo Chen,Alicia Fornés,Cristina Carmona-Duarte,Josep Lladós,Miguel A. Ferrer
title: Towards Stroke Patients' Upper-limb Automatic Motor Assessment Using Smartwatches
title_zh: 使用智能手表对中风患者上肢进行自动运动评估
abstract: Assessing the physical condition in rehabilitation scenarios is a challenging problem, since it involves Human Activity Recognition (HAR) and kinematic analysis methods. In addition, the difficulties increase in unconstrained rehabilitation scenarios, which are much closer to the real use cases. In particular, our aim is to design an upper-limb assessment pipeline for stroke patients using smartwatches. We focus on the HAR task, as it is the first part of the assessing pipeline. Our main target is to automatically detect and recognize four key movements inspired by the Fugl-Meyer assessment scale, which are performed in both constrained and unconstrained scenarios. In addition to the application protocol and dataset, we propose two detection and classification baseline methods. We believe that the proposed framework, dataset and baseline results will serve to foster this research field.
summary: Assessing the physical condition in rehabilitation scenarios is a challenging problem, since it involves Human Activity Recognition (HAR) and kinematic analysis methods. In addition, the difficulties increase in unconstrained rehabilitation scenarios, which are much closer to the real use cases. In particular, our aim is to design an upper-limb assessment pipeline for stroke patients using smartwatches. We focus on the HAR task, as it is the first part of the assessing pipeline. Our main target is to automatically detect and recognize four key movements inspired by the Fugl-Meyer assessment scale, which are performed in both constrained and unconstrained scenarios. In addition to the application protocol and dataset, we propose two detection and classification baseline methods. We believe that the proposed framework, dataset and baseline results will serve to foster this research field.
abstract_zh: 评估康复场景中的身体状况是一个具有挑战性的问题，因为它涉及人体活动识别（HAR）和运动学分析方法。此外，在无约束的康复场景中，难度会增加，这更接近真实的用例。特别是，我们的目标是使用智能手表为中风患者设计上肢评估流程。我们重点关注 HAR 任务，因为它是评估流程的第一部分。我们的主要目标是自动检测和识别受 Fugl-Meyer 评估量表启发的四个关键动作，这些动作在受约束和不受约束的场景中执行。除了应用协议和数据集之外，我们还提出了两种检测和分类基线方法。我们相信所提出的框架、数据集和基线结果将有助于促进这一研究领域的发展。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在设计基于智能手表的中风患者上肢自动运动评估系统，重点解决康复场景中的人类活动识别（HAR）问题。
AI.motivation: 传统康复评估依赖人工观察存在局限，而真实场景下非约束环境的自动评估方法仍需探索。
AI.method: 提出包含应用协议和数据集的框架，设计两种检测与分类的基线方法以识别Fugl-Meyer量表中的关键动作。
AI.result: 构建了评估框架与数据集，验证了两种基线方法在约束与非约束场景下的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该研究为中风患者上肢运动自动评估提供了技术基础与数据支持，可推动相关领域的发展。

authors_text: John Francis,Stephen Law
title: Estimating Chicago's tree cover and canopy height using multi-spectral satellite imagery
title_zh: 使用多光谱卫星图像估算芝加哥的树木覆盖率和树冠高度
comment: 4 pages, 4 figures, Submitted to Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning: workshop at NeurIPS 2022
comments: 4 pages, 4 figures, Submitted to Tackling Climate Change with Machine Learning: workshop at NeurIPS 2022
abstract: Information on urban tree canopies is fundamental to mitigating climate change [1] as well as improving quality of life [2]. Urban tree planting initiatives face a lack of up-to-date data about the horizontal and vertical dimensions of the tree canopy in cities. We present a pipeline that utilizes LiDAR data as ground-truth and then trains a multi-task machine learning model to generate reliable estimates of tree cover and canopy height in urban areas using multi-source multi-spectral satellite imagery for the case study of Chicago.
summary: Information on urban tree canopies is fundamental to mitigating climate change [1] as well as improving quality of life [2]. Urban tree planting initiatives face a lack of up-to-date data about the horizontal and vertical dimensions of the tree canopy in cities. We present a pipeline that utilizes LiDAR data as ground-truth and then trains a multi-task machine learning model to generate reliable estimates of tree cover and canopy height in urban areas using multi-source multi-spectral satellite imagery for the case study of Chicago.
abstract_zh: 有关城市树冠的信息对于缓解气候变化 [1] 以及提高生活质量 [2] 至关重要。城市植树计划面临着缺乏有关城市树冠水平和垂直尺寸的最新数据的问题。我们提出了一个管道，利用激光雷达数据作为地面实况，然后训练多任务机器学习模型，使用多源多光谱卫星图像对城市地区的树木覆盖率和树冠高度进行可靠的估计，以进行芝加哥的案例研究。

AI.tldr: 利用多光谱卫星影像估算芝加哥的树冠覆盖和树冠高度
AI.motivation: 城市树冠信息对于缓解气候变化和提升生活质量至关重要，但现有数据缺乏及时性和水平与垂直维度的详细信息
AI.method: 利用LiDAR数据作为地面真实数据，训练多任务机器学习模型，结合多源多光谱卫星影像进行估计
AI.result: 成功生成了芝加哥城市区域的树冠覆盖和高度的可靠估计
AI.conclusion: 提出了一种有效的方法，利用卫星影像和机器学习模型估计城市树冠信息，为城市绿化和气候政策提供数据支持

authors_text: Liam Magee,Vanicka Arora,Luke Munn
title: Structured Like a Language Model: Analysing AI as an Automated Subject
title_zh: 像语言模型一样构建：将人工智能作为自动化主题进行分析
abstract: Drawing from the resources of psychoanalysis and critical media studies, in this paper we develop an analysis of Large Language Models (LLMs) as automated subjects. We argue the intentional fictional projection of subjectivity onto LLMs can yield an alternate frame through which AI behaviour, including its productions of bias and harm, can be analysed. First, we introduce language models, discuss their significance and risks, and outline our case for interpreting model design and outputs with support from psychoanalytic concepts. We trace a brief history of language models, culminating with the releases, in 2022, of systems that realise state-of-the-art natural language processing performance. We engage with one such system, OpenAI's InstructGPT, as a case study, detailing the layers of its construction and conducting exploratory and semi-structured interviews with chatbots. These interviews probe the model's moral imperatives to be helpful, truthful and harmless by design. The model acts, we argue, as the condensation of often competing social desires, articulated through the internet and harvested into training data, which must then be regulated and repressed. This foundational structure can however be redirected via prompting, so that the model comes to identify with, and transfer, its commitments to the immediate human subject before it. In turn, these automated productions of language can lead to the human subject projecting agency upon the model, effecting occasionally further forms of countertransference. We conclude that critical media methods and psychoanalytic theory together offer a productive frame for grasping the powerful new capacities of AI-driven language systems.
summary: Drawing from the resources of psychoanalysis and critical media studies, in this paper we develop an analysis of Large Language Models (LLMs) as automated subjects. We argue the intentional fictional projection of subjectivity onto LLMs can yield an alternate frame through which AI behaviour, including its productions of bias and harm, can be analysed. First, we introduce language models, discuss their significance and risks, and outline our case for interpreting model design and outputs with support from psychoanalytic concepts. We trace a brief history of language models, culminating with the releases, in 2022, of systems that realise state-of-the-art natural language processing performance. We engage with one such system, OpenAI's InstructGPT, as a case study, detailing the layers of its construction and conducting exploratory and semi-structured interviews with chatbots. These interviews probe the model's moral imperatives to be helpful, truthful and harmless by design. The model acts, we argue, as the condensation of often competing social desires, articulated through the internet and harvested into training data, which must then be regulated and repressed. This foundational structure can however be redirected via prompting, so that the model comes to identify with, and transfer, its commitments to the immediate human subject before it. In turn, these automated productions of language can lead to the human subject projecting agency upon the model, effecting occasionally further forms of countertransference. We conclude that critical media methods and psychoanalytic theory together offer a productive frame for grasping the powerful new capacities of AI-driven language systems.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们借鉴精神分析和批判媒体研究的资源，对作为自动化科目的大型语言模型（LLM）进行了分析。我们认为，将主观性有意虚构地投射到法学硕士身上可以产生一个替代框架，通过该框架可以分析人工智能的行为，包括其产生的偏见和伤害。首先，我们介绍语言模型，讨论它们的重要性和风险，并概述我们在精神分析概念的支持下解释模型设计和输出的案例。我们追溯了语言模型的简史，最终于 2022 年发布了实现最先进自然语言处理性能的系统。我们将 OpenAI 的 InstructGPT 等这样一个系统作为案例研究，详细介绍了其构建的各个层次，并对聊天机器人进行了探索性和半结构化的访谈。这些采访探讨了该模型的道德要求，即通过设计来提供帮助、诚实和无害。我们认为，该模型的作用是经常相互竞争的社会欲望的凝聚，通过互联网表达出来并收集到训练数据中，然后必须对其进行监管和压制。然而，这种基本结构可以通过提示进行重定向，以便模型能够识别并将其承诺转移到它面前的直接人类主体。反过来，这些语言的自动化产生可以导致人类​​主体将代理投射到模型上，偶尔会产生进一步形式的反移情。我们的结论是，批判媒体方法和精神分析理论共同为掌握人工智能驱动的语言系统的强大新能力提供了一个富有成效的框架。

AI.tldr: 本文通过精神分析和批判媒介研究的方法，将大型语言模型（LLMs）视为自动化主体，分析其行为及潜在偏见和危害。
AI.motivation: 探讨AI行为的社会和伦理影响，通过将主体性投射到LLMs上，提供新的分析框架以理解其生成内容中的偏见与伤害。
AI.method: 结合精神分析理论与批判媒介研究，以OpenAI的InstructGPT为案例，分析其设计结构并通过半结构化访谈探索模型的道德准则。
AI.result: 发现LLMs是社会欲望的凝结，通过训练数据被规训与压抑，但可通过提示重定向其承诺，引发人类主体的代理权投射与反移情现象。
AI.conclusion: 批判媒介方法与精神分析理论结合，为理解AI驱动的语言系统提供了有力的分析框架。

search_text: Structured Like a Language Model: Analysing AI as an Automated Subject 像语言模型一样构建：将人工智能作为自动化主题进行分析 Drawing from the resources of psychoanalysis and critical media studies, in this paper we develop an analysis of Large Language Models (LLMs) as automated subjects. We argue the intentional fictional projection of subjectivity onto LLMs can yield an alternate frame through which AI behaviour, including its productions of bias and harm, can be analysed. First, we introduce language models, discuss their significance and risks, and outline our case for interpreting model design and outputs with support from psychoanalytic concepts. We trace a brief history of language models, culminating with the releases, in 2022, of systems that realise state-of-the-art natural language processing performance. We engage with one such system, OpenAI's InstructGPT, as a case study, detailing the layers of its construction and conducting exploratory and semi-structured interviews with chatbots. These interviews probe the model's moral imperatives to be helpful, truthful and harmless by design. The model acts, we argue, as the condensation of often competing social desires, articulated through the internet and harvested into training data, which must then be regulated and repressed. This foundational structure can however be redirected via prompting, so that the model comes to identify with, and transfer, its commitments to the immediate human subject before it. In turn, these automated productions of language can lead to the human subject projecting agency upon the model, effecting occasionally further forms of countertransference. We conclude that critical media methods and psychoanalytic theory together offer a productive frame for grasping the powerful new capacities of AI-driven language systems. 在本文中，我们借鉴精神分析和批判媒体研究的资源，对作为自动化科目的大型语言模型（LLM）进行了分析。我们认为，将主观性有意虚构地投射到法学硕士身上可以产生一个替代框架，通过该框架可以分析人工智能的行为，包括其产生的偏见和伤害。首先，我们介绍语言模型，讨论它们的重要性和风险，并概述我们在精神分析概念的支持下解释模型设计和输出的案例。我们追溯了语言模型的简史，最终于 2022 年发布了实现最先进自然语言处理性能的系统。我们将 OpenAI 的 InstructGPT 等这样一个系统作为案例研究，详细介绍了其构建的各个层次，并对聊天机器人进行了探索性和半结构化的访谈。这些采访探讨了该模型的道德要求，即通过设计来提供帮助、诚实和无害。我们认为，该模型的作用是经常相互竞争的社会欲望的凝聚，通过互联网表达出来并收集到训练数据中，然后必须对其进行监管和压制。然而，这种基本结构可以通过提示进行重定向，以便模型能够识别并将其承诺转移到它面前的直接人类主体。反过来，这些语言的自动化产生可以导致人类​​主体将代理投射到模型上，偶尔会产生进一步形式的反移情。我们的结论是，批判媒体方法和精神分析理论共同为掌握人工智能驱动的语言系统的强大新能力提供了一个富有成效的框架。 本文通过精神分析和批判媒介研究的方法，将大型语言模型（LLMs）视为自动化主体，分析其行为及潜在偏见和危害。 Liam Magee,Vanicka Arora,Luke Munn cs.CY,cs.AI

authors_text: Kaan Akşit,Yuta Itoh
title: HoloBeam: Paper-Thin Near-Eye Displays
title_zh: HoloBeam：薄如纸的近眼显示器
abstract: An emerging alternative to conventional Augmented Reality (AR) glasses designs, Beaming displays promise slim AR glasses free from challenging design trade-offs, including battery-related limits or computational budget-related issues. These beaming displays remove active components such as batteries and electronics from AR glasses and move them to a projector that projects images to a user from a distance (1-2 meters), where users wear only passive optical eyepieces. However, earlier implementations of these displays delivered poor resolutions (7 cycles per degree) without any optical focus cues and were introduced with a bulky form-factor eyepiece (50 mm thick). This paper introduces a new milestone for beaming displays, which we call HoloBeam. In this new design, a custom holographic projector populates a micro-volume located at some distance (1-2 meters) with multiple planes of images. Users view magnified copies of these images from this small volume with the help of an eyepiece that is either a Holographic Optical Element (HOE) or a set of lenses. Our HoloBeam prototypes demonstrate the thinnest AR glasses to date with a submillimeter thickness (e.g., HOE film is only 120 um thick). In addition, HoloBeam prototypes demonstrate near retinal resolutions (24 cycles per degree) with a 70 degrees-wide field of view.
summary: An emerging alternative to conventional Augmented Reality (AR) glasses designs, Beaming displays promise slim AR glasses free from challenging design trade-offs, including battery-related limits or computational budget-related issues. These beaming displays remove active components such as batteries and electronics from AR glasses and move them to a projector that projects images to a user from a distance (1-2 meters), where users wear only passive optical eyepieces. However, earlier implementations of these displays delivered poor resolutions (7 cycles per degree) without any optical focus cues and were introduced with a bulky form-factor eyepiece (50 mm thick). This paper introduces a new milestone for beaming displays, which we call HoloBeam. In this new design, a custom holographic projector populates a micro-volume located at some distance (1-2 meters) with multiple planes of images. Users view magnified copies of these images from this small volume with the help of an eyepiece that is either a Holographic Optical Element (HOE) or a set of lenses. Our HoloBeam prototypes demonstrate the thinnest AR glasses to date with a submillimeter thickness (e.g., HOE film is only 120 um thick). In addition, HoloBeam prototypes demonstrate near retinal resolutions (24 cycles per degree) with a 70 degrees-wide field of view.
abstract_zh: An emerging alternative to conventional Augmented Reality (AR) glasses designs, Beaming displays promise slim AR glasses free from challenging design trade-offs, including battery-related limits or computational budget-related issues. These beaming displays remove active components such as batteries and electronics from AR glasses and move them to a projector that projects images to a user from a distance (1-2 meters), where users wear only passive optical eyepieces. However, earlier implementations of these displays delivered poor resolutions (7 cycles per degree) without any optical focus cues and were introduced with a bulky form-factor eyepiece (50 mm thick).本文介绍了光束显示器的一个新里程碑，我们称之为 HoloBeam。 In this new design, a custom holographic projector populates a micro-volume located at some distance (1-2 meters) with multiple planes of images. Users view magnified copies of these images from this small volume with the help of an eyepiece that is either a Holographic Optical Element (HOE) or a set of lenses.我们的 HoloBeam 原型展示了迄今为止最薄的 AR 眼镜，厚度为亚毫米（例如，HOE 薄膜只有 120 微米厚）。此外，HoloBeam 原型还展示了接近视网膜的分辨率（每度 24 个周期）和 70 度宽的视野。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种超薄近眼显示技术HoloBeam，通过将主动电子元件移至投影仪，仅让用户佩戴被动光学目镜，解决传统AR眼镜的设计限制。
AI.motivation: 传统AR眼镜受限于电池容量和计算预算，而早期光场显示技术存在分辨率低（7周期/度）和目镜体积大（50mm厚）的缺陷，亟需突破性方案。
AI.method: 采用自定义全息投影仪在1-2米距离的小体积内生成多平面图像，通过HOE或透镜目镜实现图像放大，消除光学焦点线索的限制。
AI.result: 原型机实现亚毫米级厚度（如HOE膜厚仅120微米），视场角70度，分辨率提升至24周期/度，达到近视网膜级别。
AI.conclusion: HoloBeam在厚度和分辨率上取得双重突破，为无电池AR眼镜的实用化提供了关键技术路径。

authors_text: Sergi D. Bray,Shane D. Johnson,Bennett Kleinberg
title: Testing Human Ability To Detect Deepfake Images of Human Faces
title_zh: 测试人类检测人脸深度伪造图像的能力
abstract: Deepfakes are computationally-created entities that falsely represent reality. They can take image, video, and audio modalities, and pose a threat to many areas of systems and societies, comprising a topic of interest to various aspects of cybersecurity and cybersafety. In 2020 a workshop consulting AI experts from academia, policing, government, the private sector, and state security agencies ranked deepfakes as the most serious AI threat. These experts noted that since fake material can propagate through many uncontrolled routes, changes in citizen behaviour may be the only effective defence. This study aims to assess human ability to identify image deepfakes of human faces (StyleGAN2:FFHQ) from nondeepfake images (FFHQ), and to assess the effectiveness of simple interventions intended to improve detection accuracy. Using an online survey, 280 participants were randomly allocated to one of four groups: a control group, and 3 assistance interventions. Each participant was shown a sequence of 20 images randomly selected from a pool of 50 deepfake and 50 real images of human faces. Participants were asked if each image was AI-generated or not, to report their confidence, and to describe the reasoning behind each response. Overall detection accuracy was only just above chance and none of the interventions significantly improved this. Participants' confidence in their answers was high and unrelated to accuracy. Assessing the results on a per-image basis reveals participants consistently found certain images harder to label correctly, but reported similarly high confidence regardless of the image. Thus, although participant accuracy was 62% overall, this accuracy across images ranged quite evenly between 85% and 30%, with an accuracy of below 50% for one in every five images. We interpret the findings as suggesting that there is a need for an urgent call to action to address this threat.

Thus, although participant accuracy was 62% overall, this accuracy across images ranged quite evenly between 85% and 30%, with an accuracy of below 50% for one in every five images. We interpret the findings as suggesting that there is a need for an urgent call to action to address this threat.
abstract_zh: Deepfakes 是通过计算创建的实体，错误地代表了现实。它们可以采用图像、视频和音频形式，并对系统和社会的许多领域构成威胁，包括网络安全和网络安全各个方面感兴趣的主题。 2020 年，一个咨询来自学术界、警察、政府、私营部门和国家安全机构的人工智能专家的研讨会将深度造假列为最严重的人工智能威胁。这些专家指出，由于虚假材料可以通过许多不受控制的途径传播，因此改变公民行为可能是唯一有效的防御措施。本研究旨在评估人类从非深度伪造图像 (FFHQ) 中识别人脸深度伪造图像 (StyleGAN2:FFHQ) 的能力，并评估旨在提高检测准确性的简单干预措施的有效性。通过在线调查，280 名参与者被随机分配到四组中的一组：对照组和 3 项辅助干预措施。 Each participant was shown a sequence of 20 images randomly selected from a pool of 50 deepfake and 50 real images of human faces. Participants were asked if each image was AI-generated or not, to report their confidence, and to describe the reasoning behind each response. Overall detection accuracy was only just above chance and none of the interventions significantly improved this. Participants' confidence in their answers was high and unrelated to accuracy.对每张图像的结果进行评估表明，参与者始终发现某些图像难以正确标记，但无论图像如何，都报告了类似的高置信度。因此，尽管参与者的总体准确度为 62%，但图像的准确度相当均匀地分布在 85% 到 30% 之间，每五张图像中就有一张的准确度低于 50%。我们将调查结果解读为表明需要紧急呼吁采取行动来应对这一威胁。

AI.tldr: 评估人类识别深度伪造人脸图像的能力及简单干预措施的效果
AI.motivation: 深度伪造技术被列为最严重的AI威胁，而人类检测能力可能成为唯一有效防御手段，需验证其实际效果
AI.method: 通过在线调查让280名参与者分组识别20张随机选取的深度伪造与真实人脸图像，记录判断结果、置信度及理由
AI.result: 整体检测准确率仅62%（略高于随机），干预措施无效，参与者自信度高但与准确率无关，部分图像识别困难度与自信度不匹配
AI.conclusion: 人类检测深度伪造能力有限且易产生虚假自信，需紧急开发更有效的技术防御方案

search_text: Testing Human Ability To Detect Deepfake Images of Human Faces 测试人类检测人脸深度伪造图像的能力 Deepfakes are computationally-created entities that falsely represent reality. They can take image, video, and audio modalities, and pose a threat to many areas of systems and societies, comprising a topic of interest to various aspects of cybersecurity and cybersafety. In 2020 a workshop consulting AI experts from academia, policing, government, the private sector, and state security agencies ranked deepfakes as the most serious AI threat. These experts noted that since fake material can propagate through many uncontrolled routes, changes in citizen behaviour may be the only effective defence. This study aims to assess human ability to identify image deepfakes of human faces (StyleGAN2:FFHQ) from nondeepfake images (FFHQ), and to assess the effectiveness of simple interventions intended to improve detection accuracy. Using an online survey, 280 participants were randomly allocated to one of four groups: a control group, and 3 assistance interventions. Each participant was shown a sequence of 20 images randomly selected from a pool of 50 deepfake and 50 real images of human faces. Participants were asked if each image was AI-generated or not, to report their confidence, and to describe the reasoning behind each response. Overall detection accuracy was only just above chance and none of the interventions significantly improved this. Participants' confidence in their answers was high and unrelated to accuracy. Assessing the results on a per-image basis reveals participants consistently found certain images harder to label correctly, but reported similarly high confidence regardless of the image. Thus, although participant accuracy was 62% overall, this accuracy across images ranged quite evenly between 85% and 30%, with an accuracy of below 50% for one in every five images. We interpret the findings as suggesting that there is a need for an urgent call to action to address this threat. Participants were asked if each image was AI-generated or not, to report their confidence, and to describe the reasoning behind each response. authors_text: Aran Komatsuzaki,Joan Puigcerver,James Lee-Thorp,Carlos Riquelme Ruiz,Basil Mustafa,Joshua Ainslie,Yi Tay,Mostafa Dehghani,Neil Houlsby
title: Sparse Upcycling: Training Mixture-of-Experts from Dense Checkpoints
title_zh: 稀疏升级改造：从密集检查点培训专家组合
abstract: Training large, deep neural networks to convergence can be prohibitively expensive. As a result, often only a small selection of popular, dense models are reused across different contexts and tasks. Increasingly, sparsely activated models, which seek to decouple model size from computation costs, are becoming an attractive alternative to dense models. Although more efficient in terms of quality and computation cost, sparse models remain data-hungry and costly to train from scratch in the large scale regime. In this work, we propose sparse upcycling -- a simple way to reuse sunk training costs by initializing a sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts model from a dense checkpoint. We show that sparsely upcycled T5 Base, Large, and XL language models and Vision Transformer Base and Large models, respectively, significantly outperform their dense counterparts on SuperGLUE and ImageNet, using only ~50% of the initial dense pretraining sunk cost. The upcycled models also outperform sparse models trained from scratch on 100% of the initial dense pretraining computation budget.
summary: Training large, deep neural networks to convergence can be prohibitively expensive. As a result, often only a small selection of popular, dense models are reused across different contexts and tasks. Increasingly, sparsely activated models, which seek to decouple model size from computation costs, are becoming an attractive alternative to dense models. Although more efficient in terms of quality and computation cost, sparse models remain data-hungry and costly to train from scratch in the large scale regime. In this work, we propose sparse upcycling -- a simple way to reuse sunk training costs by initializing a sparsely activated Mixture-of-Experts model from a dense checkpoint. We show that sparsely upcycled T5 Base, Large, and XL language models and Vision Transformer Base and Large models, respectively, significantly outperform their dense counterparts on SuperGLUE and ImageNet, using only ~50% of the initial dense pretraining sunk cost. The upcycled models also outperform sparse models trained from scratch on 100% of the initial dense pretraining computation budget.
abstract_zh: 训练大型深度神经网络以实现收敛的成本可能非常昂贵。因此，通常只有一小部分流行的密集模型会在不同的上下文和任务中重复使用。越来越多的稀疏激活模型试图将模型大小与计算成本分离，正在成为密集模型的有吸引力的替代品。尽管在质量和计算成本方面更有效，但稀疏模型仍然需要大量数据，并且在大规模情况下从头开始训练成本高昂。在这项工作中，我们提出了稀疏升级——一种通过从密集检查点初始化稀疏激活的专家混合模型来重用沉没训练成本的简单方法。我们表明，稀疏升级的 T5 Base、Large 和 XL 语言模型以及 Vision Transformer Base 和 Large 模型分别在 SuperGLUE 和 ImageNet 上显着优于其密集模型，仅使用初始密集预训练沉没成本的约 50%。升级后的模型还优于在 100% 的初始密集预训练计算预算上从头开始训练的稀疏模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为Sparse Upcycling的方法，通过从密集检查点初始化稀疏激活的专家混合模型，以降低训练成本并提升性能。
AI.motivation: 训练大型深度神经网络成本高昂，而稀疏模型虽计算效率高但训练成本依然巨大，需寻找有效复用已有训练资源的方法。
AI.method: 从预训练的密集模型检查点初始化稀疏专家混合模型，通过调整激活机制实现参数高效微调。
AI.result: 在SuperGLUE和ImageNet任务中，稀疏升级的T5和Vision Transformer模型以约50%的原始训练成本超越密集模型，且优于从头训练的稀疏模型。
AI.conclusion: Sparse Upcycling有效复用密集训练成本，为大规模稀疏模型训练提供了高效且性能优越的解决方案。

authors_text: Feng Cheng,Xizi Wang,Jie Lei,David Crandall,Mohit Bansal,Gedas Bertasius
title: VindLU: A Recipe for Effective Video-and-Language Pretraining
title_zh: VindLU：有效视频和语言预训练的秘诀
abstract: The last several years have witnessed remarkable progress in video-and-language (VidL) understanding. However, most modern VidL approaches use complex and specialized model architectures and sophisticated pretraining protocols, making the reproducibility, analysis and comparisons of these frameworks difficult. Hence, instead of proposing yet another new VidL model, this paper conducts a thorough empirical study demystifying the most important factors in the VidL model design. Among the factors that we investigate are (i) the spatiotemporal architecture design, (ii) the multimodal fusion schemes, (iii) the pretraining objectives, (iv) the choice of pretraining data, (v) pretraining and finetuning protocols, and (vi) dataset and model scaling. Our empirical study reveals that the most important design factors include: temporal modeling, video-to-text multimodal fusion, masked modeling objectives, and joint training on images and videos. Using these empirical insights, we then develop a step-by-step recipe, dubbed VindLU, for effective VidL pretraining. Our final model trained using our recipe achieves comparable or better than state-of-the-art results on several VidL tasks without relying on external CLIP pretraining. In particular, on the text-to-video retrieval task, our approach obtains 61.2% on DiDeMo, and 55.0% on ActivityNet, outperforming current SOTA by 7.8% and 6.1% respectively. Furthermore, our model also obtains state-of-the-art video question-answering results on ActivityNet-QA, MSRVTT-QA, MSRVTT-MC and TVQA. Our code and pretrained models are publicly available at: https://github.com/klauscc/VindLU.
summary: The last several years have witnessed remarkable progress in video-and-language (VidL) understanding. However, most modern VidL approaches use complex and specialized model architectures and sophisticated pretraining protocols, making the reproducibility, analysis and comparisons of these frameworks difficult. Hence, instead of proposing yet another new VidL model, this paper conducts a thorough empirical study demystifying the most important factors in the VidL model design. Among the factors that we investigate are (i) the spatiotemporal architecture design, (ii) the multimodal fusion schemes, (iii) the pretraining objectives, (iv) the choice of pretraining data, (v) pretraining and finetuning protocols, and (vi) dataset and model scaling. Our empirical study reveals that the most important design factors include: temporal modeling, video-to-text multimodal fusion, masked modeling objectives, and joint training on images and videos. Using these empirical insights, we then develop a step-by-step recipe, dubbed VindLU, for effective VidL pretraining. Our final model trained using our recipe achieves comparable or better than state-of-the-art results on several VidL tasks without relying on external CLIP pretraining. In particular, on the text-to-video retrieval task, our approach obtains 61.2% on DiDeMo, and 55.0% on ActivityNet, outperforming current SOTA by 7.8% and 6.1% respectively. Furthermore, our model also obtains state-of-the-art video question-answering results on ActivityNet-QA, MSRVTT-QA, MSRVTT-MC and TVQA. Our code and pretrained models are publicly available at: https://github.com/klauscc/VindLU.
abstract_zh: 过去几年见证了视频和语言（VidL）理解方面的显着进步。然而，大多数现代 VidL 方法使用复杂且专门的模型架构和复杂的预训练协议，使得这些框架的再现性、分析和比较变得困难。因此，本文没有提出另一个新的 VidL 模型，而是进行了彻底的实证研究，揭开了 VidL 模型设计中最重要因素的神秘面纱。我们研究的因素包括（i）时空架构设计，（ii）多模态融合方案，（iii）预训练目标，（iv）预训练数据的选择，（v）预训练和微调协议，以及（vi）数据集和模型缩放。我们的实证研究表明，最重要的设计因素包括：时间建模、视频到文本多模态融合、屏蔽建模目标以及图像和视频的联合训练。利用这些经验见解，我们开发了一个名为 VindLU 的分步方案，用于有效的 VidL 预训练。使用我们的配方训练的最终模型在多个 VidL 任务上取得了与最先进的结果相当或更好的结果，而无需依赖外部 CLIP 预训练。特别是，在文本到视频检索任务上，我们的方法在 DiDeMo 上获得了 61.2%，在 ActivityNet 上获得了 55.0%，分别比当前的 SOTA 提高了 7.8% 和 6.1%。此外，我们的模型还在 ActivityNet-QA、MSRVTT-QA、MSRVTT-MC 和 TVQA 上获得了最先进的视频问答结果。我们的代码和预训练模型可在以下网址公开获取：https://github.com/klauscc/VindLU。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为VindLU的视频-语言预训练方法，通过实证研究确定关键设计因素并开发有效预训练方案。
AI.motivation: 现有视频-语言理解方法依赖复杂架构和预训练协议，导致框架复现和比较困难，需通过实证研究简化设计并揭示关键因素。
AI.method: 通过分析时空架构、多模态融合、预训练目标等六个因素，结合实证结果提出包含时序建模、视频-文本融合等核心要素的VindLU预训练方案。
AI.result: 在DiDeMo和ActivityNet等数据集上，文本到视频检索任务分别达到61.2%和55.0%的准确率，超越现有最优方法；在多个视频问答任务中也取得SOTA结果。
AI.conclusion: 视频-语言预训练应聚焦时序建模、多模态融合及联合训练，无需依赖CLIP预训练即可实现竞争力，VindLU方案为领域提供有效实践指南。

search_text: VindLU: A Recipe for Effective Video-and-Language Pretraining VindLU：有效视频和语言预训练的秘诀 The last several years have witnessed remarkable progress in video-and-language (VidL) understanding. However, most modern VidL approaches use complex and specialized model architectures and sophisticated pretraining protocols, making the reproducibility, analysis and comparisons of these frameworks difficult. Hence, instead of proposing yet another new VidL model, this paper conducts a thorough empirical study demystifying the most important factors in the VidL model design. Among the factors that we investigate are (i) the spatiotemporal architecture design, (ii) the multimodal fusion schemes, (iii) the pretraining objectives, (iv) the choice of pretraining data, (v) pretraining and finetuning protocols, and (vi) dataset and model scaling. Our empirical study reveals that the most important design factors include: temporal modeling, video-to-text multimodal fusion, masked modeling objectives, and joint training on images and videos. Using these empirical insights, we then develop a step-by-step recipe, dubbed VindLU, for effective VidL pretraining. Our final model trained using our recipe achieves comparable or better than state-of-the-art results on several VidL tasks without relying on external CLIP pretraining. In particular, on the text-to-video retrieval task, our approach obtains 61.2% on DiDeMo, and 55.0% on ActivityNet, outperforming current SOTA by 7.8% and 6.1% respectively. Furthermore, our model also obtains state-of-the-art video question-answering results on ActivityNet-QA, MSRVTT-QA, MSRVTT-MC and TVQA. Our code and pretrained models are publicly available at: https://github.com/klauscc/VindLU. 过去几年见证了视频和语言（VidL）理解方面的显着进步。然而，大多数现代 VidL 方法使用复杂且专门的模型架构和复杂的预训练协议，使得这些框架的再现性、分析和比较变得困难。因此，本文没有提出另一个新的 VidL 模型，而是进行了彻底的实证研究，揭开了 VidL 模型设计中最重要因素的神秘面纱。我们研究的因素包括（i）时空架构设计，（ii）多模态融合方案，（iii）预训练目标，（iv）预训练数据的选择，（v）预训练和微调协议，以及（vi）数据集和模型缩放。我们的实证研究表明，最重要的设计因素包括：时间建模、视频到文本多模态融合、屏蔽建模目标以及图像和视频的联合训练。利用这些经验见解，我们开发了一个名为 VindLU 的分步方案，用于有效的 VidL 预训练。使用我们的配方训练的最终模型在多个 VidL 任务上取得了与最先进的结果相当或更好的结果，而无需依赖外部 CLIP 预训练。特别是，在文本到视频检索任务上，我们的方法在 DiDeMo 上获得了 61.2%，在 ActivityNet 上获得了 55.0%，分别比当前的 SOTA 提高了 7.8% 和 6.1%。此外，我们的模型还在 ActivityNet-QA、MSRVTT-QA、MSRVTT-MC 和 TVQA 上获得了最先进的视频问答结果。我们的代码和预训练模型可在以下网址公开获取：https://github.com/klauscc/VindLU。 本文提出一种名为VindLU的视频-语言预训练方法，通过实证研究确定关键设计因素并开发有效预训练方案。 Feng Cheng,Xizi Wang,Jie Lei,David Crandall,Mohit Bansal,Gedas Bertasius cs.CV

authors_text: Jay Bhanushali,Manivannan Muniyandi,Praneeth Chakravarthula
title: Cross-Domain Synthetic-to-Real In-the-Wild Depth and Normal Estimation for 3D Scene Understanding
title_zh: 用于 3D 场景理解的跨域合成到真实野外深度和法线估计
abstract: We present a cross-domain inference technique that learns from synthetic data to estimate depth and normals for in-the-wild omnidirectional 3D scenes encountered in real-world uncontrolled settings. To this end, we introduce UBotNet, an architecture that combines UNet and Bottleneck Transformer elements to predict consistent scene normals and depth. We also introduce the OmniHorizon synthetic dataset containing 24,335 omnidirectional images that represent a wide variety of outdoor environments, including buildings, streets, and diverse vegetation. This dataset is generated from expansive, lifelike virtual spaces and encompasses dynamic scene elements, such as changing lighting conditions, different times of day, pedestrians, and vehicles. Our experiments show that UBotNet achieves significantly improved accuracy in depth estimation and normal estimation compared to existing models. Lastly, we validate cross-domain synthetic-to-real depth and normal estimation on real outdoor images using UBotNet trained solely on our synthetic OmniHorizon dataset, demonstrating the potential of both the synthetic dataset and the proposed network for real-world scene understanding applications.
summary: We present a cross-domain inference technique that learns from synthetic data to estimate depth and normals for in-the-wild omnidirectional 3D scenes encountered in real-world uncontrolled settings. To this end, we introduce UBotNet, an architecture that combines UNet and Bottleneck Transformer elements to predict consistent scene normals and depth. We also introduce the OmniHorizon synthetic dataset containing 24,335 omnidirectional images that represent a wide variety of outdoor environments, including buildings, streets, and diverse vegetation. This dataset is generated from expansive, lifelike virtual spaces and encompasses dynamic scene elements, such as changing lighting conditions, different times of day, pedestrians, and vehicles. Our experiments show that UBotNet achieves significantly improved accuracy in depth estimation and normal estimation compared to existing models. Lastly, we validate cross-domain synthetic-to-real depth and normal estimation on real outdoor images using UBotNet trained solely on our synthetic OmniHorizon dataset, demonstrating the potential of both the synthetic dataset and the proposed network for real-world scene understanding applications.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种跨域推理技术，可以从合成数据中学习，估计在现实世界不受控制的环境中遇到的野外全向 3D 场景的深度和法线。为此，我们引入了 UBotNet，这是一种结合了 UNet 和 Bottleneck Transformer 元素的架构，可以预测一致的场景法线和深度。我们还介绍了 OmniHorizo​​n 合成数据集，其中包含 24,335 张全向图像，这些图像代表了各种户外环境，包括建筑物、街道和各种植被。该数据集是从广阔、逼真的虚拟空间生成的，包含动态场景元素，例如不断变化的照明条件、一天中的不同时间、行人和车辆。我们的实验表明，与现有模型相比，UBotNet 在深度估计和法线估计方面实现了显着提高的准确性。最后，我们使用仅在我们的合成 OmniHorizo​​n 数据集上训练的 UBotNet 验证真实户外图像的跨域合成到真实深度和法线估计，展示了合成数据集和所提出的网络在现实世界场景理解应用中的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种跨领域合成到真实场景的深度与法线估计方法，用于提升真实世界3D场景理解。
AI.motivation: 真实世界3D场景数据标注成本高且难以获取，而合成数据可提供丰富场景但存在领域差异，需解决跨领域泛化问题。
AI.method: 设计UBotNet网络架构（结合UNet与瓶颈Transformer），并构建包含24,335张全景图像的OmniHorizon合成数据集，模拟多变的户外环境。
AI.result: UBotNet在深度和法线估计任务中显著优于现有模型，且仅用合成数据训练即可在真实户外图像上实现有效跨领域推理。
AI.conclusion: 合成数据集与UBotNet架构为真实场景3D理解提供了可行方案，证明了跨领域迁移学习的有效性。

authors_text: Edward C. Mitchell,Brittany Story,David Boothe,Piotr J. Franaszczuk,Vasileios Maroulas
title: A Topological Deep Learning Framework for Neural Spike Decoding
title_zh: 用于神经脉冲解码的拓扑深度学习框架
abstract: The brain's spatial orientation system uses different neuron ensembles to aid in environment-based navigation. Two of the ways brains encode spatial information is through head direction cells and grid cells. Brains use head direction cells to determine orientation whereas grid cells consist of layers of decked neurons that overlay to provide environment-based navigation. These neurons fire in ensembles where several neurons fire at once to activate a single head direction or grid. We want to capture this firing structure and use it to decode head direction grid cell data. Understanding, representing, and decoding these neural structures requires models that encompass higher order connectivity, more than the 1-dimensional connectivity that traditional graph-based models provide. To that end, in this work, we develop a topological deep learning framework for neural spike train decoding. Our framework combines unsupervised simplicial complex discovery with the power of deep learning via a new architecture we develop herein called a simplicial convolutional recurrent neural network. Simplicial complexes, topological spaces that use not only vertices and edges but also higher-dimensional objects, naturally generalize graphs and capture more than just pairwise relationships. Additionally, this approach does not require prior knowledge of the neural activity beyond spike counts, which removes the need for similarity measurements. The effectiveness and versatility of the simplicial convolutional neural network is demonstrated on head direction and trajectory prediction via head direction and grid cell datasets.
summary: The brain's spatial orientation system uses different neuron ensembles to aid in environment-based navigation. Two of the ways brains encode spatial information is through head direction cells and grid cells. Brains use head direction cells to determine orientation whereas grid cells consist of layers of decked neurons that overlay to provide environment-based navigation. These neurons fire in ensembles where several neurons fire at once to activate a single head direction or grid. We want to capture this firing structure and use it to decode head direction grid cell data. Understanding, representing, and decoding these neural structures requires models that encompass higher order connectivity, more than the 1-dimensional connectivity that traditional graph-based models provide. To that end, in this work, we develop a topological deep learning framework for neural spike train decoding. Our framework combines unsupervised simplicial complex discovery with the power of deep learning via a new architecture we develop herein called a simplicial convolutional recurrent neural network. Simplicial complexes, topological spaces that use not only vertices and edges but also higher-dimensional objects, naturally generalize graphs and capture more than just pairwise relationships. Additionally, this approach does not require prior knowledge of the neural activity beyond spike counts, which removes the need for similarity measurements. The effectiveness and versatility of the simplicial convolutional neural network is demonstrated on head direction and trajectory prediction via head direction and grid cell datasets.
abstract_zh: 大脑的空间定向系统使用不同的神经元集合来辅助基于环境的导航。大脑编码空间信息的两种方式是通过头部方向细胞和网格细胞。大脑使用头部方向细胞来确定方向，而网格细胞由多层神经元组成，这些神经元叠加在一起以提供基于环境的导航。这些神经元集体放电，其中多个神经元同时放电以激活单个头部方向或网格。我们想要捕获这个发射结构并用它来解码头部方向网格单元数据。理解、表示和解码这些神经结构需要包含更高阶连接的模型，而不是传统基于图的模型提供的一维连接。为此，在这项工作中，我们开发了一个用于神经脉冲序列解码的拓扑深度学习框架。我们的框架通过我们在此开发的称为单纯卷积循环神经网络的新架构，将无监督单纯复杂发现与深度学习的力量结合起来。单纯复形、拓扑空间不仅使用顶点和边，还使用高维对象，自然地概括图并捕获不仅仅是成对关系。此外，这种方法不需要尖峰计数之外的神经活动的先验知识，这消除了对相似性测量的需要。通过头部方向和网格单元数据集在头部方向和轨迹预测上证明了单纯卷积神经网络的有效性和多功能性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于拓扑深度学习的框架，用于解码神经元尖峰数据，特别是头部方向和网格细胞的活动。
AI.motivation: 传统图模型仅能捕捉成对关系，无法表征大脑中神经元集合的高阶连接特性，而头部方向细胞和网格细胞的活动需要更高维度的拓扑结构建模。
AI.method: 开发了一种称为单纯形卷积循环神经网络（SCRNN）的新架构，结合无监督单纯形复形发现与深度学习，利用高维拓扑空间建模神经元群体放电模式。
AI.result: 该框架在头部方向解码和轨迹预测任务中验证了有效性，能够无需先验相似性度量仅通过尖峰计数实现高精度解码。
AI.conclusion: 单纯形卷积神经网络为神经编码解码提供了新范式，通过拓扑结构建模成功捕捉了神经元群体的高阶关联特性。

search_text: A Topological Deep Learning Framework for Neural Spike Decoding 用于神经脉冲解码的拓扑深度学习框架 The brain's spatial orientation system uses different neuron ensembles to aid in environment-based navigation. Two of the ways brains encode spatial information is through head direction cells and grid cells. Brains use head direction cells to determine orientation whereas grid cells consist of layers of decked neurons that overlay to provide environment-based navigation. These neurons fire in ensembles where several neurons fire at once to activate a single head direction or grid. We want to capture this firing structure and use it to decode head direction grid cell data. Understanding, representing, and decoding these neural structures requires models that encompass higher order connectivity, more than the 1-dimensional connectivity that traditional graph-based models provide. To that end, in this work, we develop a topological deep learning framework for neural spike train decoding. Our framework combines unsupervised simplicial complex discovery with the power of deep learning via a new architecture we develop herein called a simplicial convolutional recurrent neural network. Simplicial complexes, topological spaces that use not only vertices and edges but also higher-dimensional objects, naturally generalize graphs and capture more than just pairwise relationships. Additionally, this approach does not require prior knowledge of the neural activity beyond spike counts, which removes the need for similarity measurements. The effectiveness and versatility of the simplicial convolutional neural network is demonstrated on head direction and trajectory prediction via head direction and grid cell datasets. 大脑的空间定向系统使用不同的神经元集合来辅助基于环境的导航。大脑编码空间信息的两种方式是通过头部方向细胞和网格细胞。大脑使用头部方向细胞来确定方向，而网格细胞由多层神经元组成，这些神经元叠加在一起以提供基于环境的导航。这些神经元集体放电，其中多个神经元同时放电以激活单个头部方向或网格。我们想要捕获这个发射结构并用它来解码头部方向网格单元数据。理解、表示和解码这些神经结构需要包含更高阶连接的模型，而不是传统基于图的模型提供的一维连接。为此，在这项工作中，我们开发了一个用于神经脉冲序列解码的拓扑深度学习框架。我们的框架通过我们在此开发的称为单纯卷积循环神经网络的新架构，将无监督单纯复杂发现与深度学习的力量结合起来。单纯复形、拓扑空间不仅使用顶点和边，还使用高维对象，自然地概括图并捕获不仅仅是成对关系。此外，这种方法不需要尖峰计数之外的神经活动的先验知识，这消除了对相似性测量的需要。通过头部方向和网格单元数据集在头部方向和轨迹预测上证明了单纯卷积神经网络的有效性和多功能性。 本文提出了一种基于拓扑深度学习的框架，用于解码神经元尖峰数据，特别是头部方向和网格细胞的活动。 Edward C. Mitchell,Brittany Story,David Boothe,Piotr J. Franaszczuk,Vasileios Maroulas cs.NE,cs.LG,stat.ML

authors_text: Shaoan Xie,Zhifei Zhang,Zhe Lin,Tobias Hinz,Kun Zhang
title: SmartBrush: Text and Shape Guided Object Inpainting with Diffusion Model
title_zh: SmartBrush：使用扩散模型进行文本和形状引导的对象修复
abstract: Generic image inpainting aims to complete a corrupted image by borrowing surrounding information, which barely generates novel content. By contrast, multi-modal inpainting provides more flexible and useful controls on the inpainted content, \eg, a text prompt can be used to describe an object with richer attributes, and a mask can be used to constrain the shape of the inpainted object rather than being only considered as a missing area. We propose a new diffusion-based model named SmartBrush for completing a missing region with an object using both text and shape-guidance. While previous work such as DALLE-2 and Stable Diffusion can do text-guided inapinting they do not support shape guidance and tend to modify background texture surrounding the generated object. Our model incorporates both text and shape guidance with precision control. To preserve the background better, we propose a novel training and sampling strategy by augmenting the diffusion U-net with object-mask prediction. Lastly, we introduce a multi-task training strategy by jointly training inpainting with text-to-image generation to leverage more training data. We conduct extensive experiments showing that our model outperforms all baselines in terms of visual quality, mask controllability, and background preservation.
summary: Generic image inpainting aims to complete a corrupted image by borrowing surrounding information, which barely generates novel content. By contrast, multi-modal inpainting provides more flexible and useful controls on the inpainted content, \eg, a text prompt can be used to describe an object with richer attributes, and a mask can be used to constrain the shape of the inpainted object rather than being only considered as a missing area. We propose a new diffusion-based model named SmartBrush for completing a missing region with an object using both text and shape-guidance. While previous work such as DALLE-2 and Stable Diffusion can do text-guided inapinting they do not support shape guidance and tend to modify background texture surrounding the generated object. Our model incorporates both text and shape guidance with precision control. To preserve the background better, we propose a novel training and sampling strategy by augmenting the diffusion U-net with object-mask prediction. Lastly, we introduce a multi-task training strategy by jointly training inpainting with text-to-image generation to leverage more training data. We conduct extensive experiments showing that our model outperforms all baselines in terms of visual quality, mask controllability, and background preservation.
abstract_zh: 通用图像修复旨在通过借用周围的信息来完成损坏的图像，这几乎不会产生新的内容。相比之下，多模态修复对修复内容提供了更加灵活和有用的控制，例如可以使用文本提示来描述具有更丰富属性的对象，并且可以使用遮罩来约束修复对象的形状而不是仅仅将其视为缺失区域。我们提出了一种名为 SmartBrush 的新的基于扩散的模型，用于使用文本和形状引导来完成对象的缺失区域。虽然 DALLE-2 和 Stable Diffusion 等之前的工作可以在绘图中进行文本引导，但它们不支持形状引导，并且倾向于修改生成对象周围的背景纹理。我们的模型将文本和形状引导与精确控制结合在一起。为了更好地保留背景，我们提出了一种新颖的训练和采样策略，通过使用对象掩模预测增强扩散 U 网。最后，我们引入了一种多任务训练策略，通过联合训练修复和文本到图像生成来利用更多的训练数据。我们进行了大量的实验，表明我们的模型在视觉质量、掩模可控性和背景保留方面优于所有基线。

AI.tldr: 提出一种结合文本和形状引导的扩散模型，用于更精确的图像修复
AI.motivation: 现有方法缺乏形状控制且背景易受损，需更精确的修复方法
AI.method: 改进扩散U-Net以预测物体掩码，结合文本和形状引导，并采用多任务训练策略
AI.result: 实验表明在视觉质量、掩码控制和背景保留方面优于现有方法
AI.conclusion: 结合文本和形状引导的扩散模型能有效提升图像修复的精度和质量
