authors_text: Yunzhi Zhang,Shangzhe Wu,Noah Snavely,Jiajun Wu
title: Seeing a Rose in Five Thousand Ways
title_zh: 看玫瑰五千种方式
abstract: What is a rose, visually? A rose comprises its intrinsics, including the distribution of geometry, texture, and material specific to its object category. With knowledge of these intrinsic properties, we may render roses of different sizes and shapes, in different poses, and under different lighting conditions. In this work, we build a generative model that learns to capture such object intrinsics from a single image, such as a photo of a bouquet. Such an image includes multiple instances of an object type. These instances all share the same intrinsics, but appear different due to a combination of variance within these intrinsics and differences in extrinsic factors, such as pose and illumination. Experiments show that our model successfully learns object intrinsics (distribution of geometry, texture, and material) for a wide range of objects, each from a single Internet image. Our method achieves superior results on multiple downstream tasks, including intrinsic image decomposition, shape and image generation, view synthesis, and relighting.
summary: What is a rose, visually? A rose comprises its intrinsics, including the distribution of geometry, texture, and material specific to its object category. With knowledge of these intrinsic properties, we may render roses of different sizes and shapes, in different poses, and under different lighting conditions. In this work, we build a generative model that learns to capture such object intrinsics from a single image, such as a photo of a bouquet. Such an image includes multiple instances of an object type. These instances all share the same intrinsics, but appear different due to a combination of variance within these intrinsics and differences in extrinsic factors, such as pose and illumination. Experiments show that our model successfully learns object intrinsics (distribution of geometry, texture, and material) for a wide range of objects, each from a single Internet image. Our method achieves superior results on multiple downstream tasks, including intrinsic image decomposition, shape and image generation, view synthesis, and relighting.
abstract_zh: What is a rose, visually?玫瑰由其内在组成，包括特定于其对象类别的几何形状、纹理和材料的分布。 With knowledge of these intrinsic properties, we may render roses of different sizes and shapes, in different poses, and under different lighting conditions.在这项工作中，我们构建了一个生成模型，该模型学习从单个图像（例如花束照片）中捕获此类对象的内在特征。这样的图像包括对象类型的多个实例。 These instances all share the same intrinsics, but appear different due to a combination of variance within these intrinsics and differences in extrinsic factors, such as pose and illumination. Experiments show that our model successfully learns object intrinsics (distribution of geometry, texture, and material) for a wide range of objects, each from a single Internet image. Our method achieves superior results on multiple downstream tasks, including intrinsic image decomposition, shape and image generation, view synthesis, and relighting.

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种从单张图像中学习物体内在属性（几何、纹理、材质）的生成模型，用于生成不同形态、光照条件下的物体。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以从单张图像中分离物体的内在属性与外在因素（如光照、姿态），而实际应用中需要从有限数据中生成多样化物体表现。
AI.method: 构建生成模型通过多实例学习，从单张含多个物体的图像中分解共享的内在属性，并分离外在因素影响。
AI.result: 模型在内在图像分解、形状生成、视图合成和重新照明等任务中优于现有方法，适用于多种物体类别。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了从单图学习物体内在属性的可行性，为生成模型提供了新思路，并在多项任务中展现优势。

authors_text: Chiraphat Boonnag,Wanumaidah Saengmolee,Narongrid Seesawad,Amrest Chinkamol,Saendee Rattanasomrerk,Kanyakorn Veerakanjana,Kamonwan Thanontip,Warissara Limpornchitwilai,Piyalitt Ittichaiwong,Theerawit Wilaiprasitporn
title: PACMAN: a framework for pulse oximeter digit detection and reading in a low-resource setting
title_zh: PACMAN：在资源匮乏的情况下进行脉搏血氧计数字检测和读取的框架
abstract: In light of the COVID-19 pandemic, patients were required to manually input their daily oxygen saturation (SpO2) and pulse rate (PR) values into a health monitoring system-unfortunately, such a process trend to be an error in typing. Several studies attempted to detect the physiological value from the captured image using optical character recognition (OCR). However, the technology has limited availability with high cost. Thus, this study aimed to propose a novel framework called PACMAN (Pandemic Accelerated Human-Machine Collaboration) with a low-resource deep learning-based computer vision. We compared state-of-the-art object detection algorithms (scaled YOLOv4, YOLOv5, and YOLOR), including the commercial OCR tools for digit recognition on the captured images from pulse oximeter display. All images were derived from crowdsourced data collection with varying quality and alignment. YOLOv5 was the best-performing model against the given model comparison across all datasets, notably the correctly orientated image dataset. We further improved the model performance with the digits auto-orientation algorithm and applied a clustering algorithm to extract SpO2 and PR values. The accuracy performance of YOLOv5 with the implementations was approximately 81.0-89.5%, which was enhanced compared to without any additional implementation. Accordingly, this study highlighted the completion of PACMAN framework to detect and read digits in real-world datasets. The proposed framework has been currently integrated into the patient monitoring system utilized by hospitals nationwide.
summary: In light of the COVID-19 pandemic, patients were required to manually input their daily oxygen saturation (SpO2) and pulse rate (PR) values into a health monitoring system-unfortunately, such a process trend to be an error in typing. Several studies attempted to detect the physiological value from the captured image using optical character recognition (OCR). However, the technology has limited availability with high cost. Thus, this study aimed to propose a novel framework called PACMAN (Pandemic Accelerated Human-Machine Collaboration) with a low-resource deep learning-based computer vision. We compared state-of-the-art object detection algorithms (scaled YOLOv4, YOLOv5, and YOLOR), including the commercial OCR tools for digit recognition on the captured images from pulse oximeter display. All images were derived from crowdsourced data collection with varying quality and alignment. YOLOv5 was the best-performing model against the given model comparison across all datasets, notably the correctly orientated image dataset. We further improved the model performance with the digits auto-orientation algorithm and applied a clustering algorithm to extract SpO2 and PR values. The accuracy performance of YOLOv5 with the implementations was approximately 81.0-89.5%, which was enhanced compared to without any additional implementation. Accordingly, this study highlighted the completion of PACMAN framework to detect and read digits in real-world datasets. The proposed framework has been currently integrated into the patient monitoring system utilized by hospitals nationwide.
abstract_zh: 鉴于 COVID-19 大流行，患者需要手动将每日血氧饱和度 (SpO2) 和脉搏率 (PR) 值输入健康监测系统 - 不幸的是，这样的过程趋势是打字错误。多项研究尝试使用光学字符识别 (OCR) 从捕获的图像中检测生理值。然而，该技术的可用性有限且成本高昂。因此，本研究旨在提出一种名为 PACMAN（流行病加速人机协作）的新颖框架，该框架具有基于低资源深度学习的计算机视觉。我们比较了最先进的对象检测算法（缩放的 YOLOv4、YOLOv5 和 YOLOR），包括用于对脉搏血氧计显示屏捕获的图像进行数字识别的商业 OCR 工具。所有图像均来自众包数据收集，具有不同的质量和对齐方式。与所有数据集（尤其是正确定向的图像数据集）的给定模型比较相比，YOLOv5 是性能最佳的模型。我们通过数字自动定向算法进一步提高了模型性能，并应用聚类算法提取 SpO2 和 PR 值。实施后的 YOLOv5 的准确率约为 81.0-89.5%，与没有任何额外实施的情况相比有所提高。因此，这项研究强调了 PACMAN 框架的完成，用于检测和读取现实数据集中的数字。所提出的框架目前已集成到全国医院使用的患者监测系统中。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于低资源深度学习的PACMAN框架，用于在真实世界数据集中检测和读取脉搏血氧仪的数字。
AI.motivation: 新冠疫情期间手动输入血氧饱和度和脉搏率存在输入错误，而现有OCR技术成本高且效果有限，需开发低成本解决方案。
AI.method: 比较YOLOv4/YOLOv5/YOLOR等目标检测算法及OCR工具，结合自动方向校正和聚类算法优化模型性能。
AI.result: YOLOv5在实施后准确率达81.0-89.5%，优于未优化模型，框架已整合至全国医院监测系统。
AI.conclusion: PACMAN框架有效解决了低资源环境下血氧仪数字识别问题，具有实际应用价值。

search_text: PACMAN: a framework for pulse oximeter digit detection and reading in a low-resource setting PACMAN：在资源匮乏的情况下进行脉搏血氧计数字检测和读取的框架 In light of the COVID-19 pandemic, patients were required to manually input their daily oxygen saturation (SpO2) and pulse rate (PR) values into a health monitoring system-unfortunately, such a process trend to be an error in typing. Several studies attempted to detect the physiological value from the captured image using optical character recognition (OCR). However, the technology has limited availability with high cost. Thus, this study aimed to propose a novel framework called PACMAN (Pandemic Accelerated Human-Machine Collaboration) with a low-resource deep learning-based computer vision. We compared state-of-the-art object detection algorithms (scaled YOLOv4, YOLOv5, and YOLOR), including the commercial OCR tools for digit recognition on the captured images from pulse oximeter display. All images were derived from crowdsourced data collection with varying quality and alignment. YOLOv5 was the best-performing model against the given model comparison across all datasets, notably the correctly orientated image dataset. We further improved the model performance with the digits auto-orientation algorithm and applied a clustering algorithm to extract SpO2 and PR values. The accuracy performance of YOLOv5 with the implementations was approximately 81.0-89.5%, which was enhanced compared to without any additional implementation. Accordingly, this study highlighted the completion of PACMAN framework to detect and read digits in real-world datasets. The proposed framework has been currently integrated into the patient monitoring system utilized by hospitals nationwide. 鉴于 COVID-19 大流行，患者需要手动将每日血氧饱和度 (SpO2) 和脉搏率 (PR) 值输入健康监测系统 - 不幸的是，这样的过程趋势是打字错误。多项研究尝试使用光学字符识别 (OCR) 从捕获的图像中检测生理值。然而，该技术的可用性有限且成本高昂。因此，本研究旨在提出一种名为 PACMAN（流行病加速人机协作）的新颖框架，该框架具有基于低资源深度学习的计算机视觉。我们比较了最先进的对象检测算法（缩放的 YOLOv4、YOLOv5 和 YOLOR），包括用于对脉搏血氧计显示屏捕获的图像进行数字识别的商业 OCR 工具。所有图像均来自众包数据收集，具有不同的质量和对齐方式。与所有数据集（尤其是正确定向的图像数据集）的给定模型比较相比，YOLOv5 是性能最佳的模型。我们通过数字自动定向算法进一步提高了模型性能，并应用聚类算法提取 SpO2 和 PR 值。实施后的 YOLOv5 的准确率约为 81.0-89.5%，与没有任何额外实施的情况相比有所提高。因此，这项研究强调了 PACMAN 框架的完成，用于检测和读取现实数据集中的数字。所提出的框架目前已集成到全国医院使用的患者监测系统中。 本文提出一种基于低资源深度学习的PACMAN框架，用于在真实世界数据集中检测和读取脉搏血氧仪的数字。 Chiraphat Boonnag,Wanumaidah Saengmolee,Narongrid Seesawad,Amrest Chinkamol,Saendee Rattanasomrerk,Kanyakorn Veerakanjana,Kamonwan Thanontip,Warissara Limpornchitwilai,Piyalitt Ittichaiwong,Theerawit Wilaiprasitporn eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Meryem Banu Cavlak,Gagandeep Singh,Mohammed Alser,Can Firtina,Joël Lindegger,Mohammad Sadrosadati,Nika Mansouri Ghiasi,Can Alkan,Onur Mutlu
title: TargetCall: Eliminating the Wasted Computation in Basecalling via Pre-Basecalling Filtering
title_zh: TargetCall：通过预碱基调用过滤消除碱基调用中的计算浪费
abstract: Basecalling is an essential step in nanopore sequencing analysis where the raw signals of nanopore sequencers are converted into nucleotide sequences, i.e., reads. State-of-the-art basecallers employ complex deep learning models to achieve high basecalling accuracy. This makes basecalling computationally inefficient and memory-hungry, bottlenecking the entire genome analysis pipeline. However, for many applications, the majority of reads do no match the reference genome of interest (i.e., target reference) and thus are discarded in later steps in the genomics pipeline, wasting the basecalling computation. To overcome this issue, we propose TargetCall, the first pre-basecalling filter to eliminate the wasted computation in basecalling. TargetCall's key idea is to discard reads that will not match the target reference (i.e., off-target reads) prior to basecalling. TargetCall consists of two main components: (1) LightCall, a lightweight neural network basecaller that produces noisy reads; and (2) Similarity Check, which labels each of these noisy reads as on-target or off-target by matching them to the target reference. Our thorough experimental evaluations show that TargetCall 1) improves the end-to-end basecalling runtime performance of the state-of-the-art basecaller by 3.31x while maintaining high (98.88%) recall in keeping on-target reads, 2) maintains high accuracy in downstream analysis, and 3) achieves better runtime performance, throughput, recall, precision, and generality compared to prior works. TargetCall is available at https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/TargetCall.
summary: Basecalling is an essential step in nanopore sequencing analysis where the raw signals of nanopore sequencers are converted into nucleotide sequences, i.e., reads. State-of-the-art basecallers employ complex deep learning models to achieve high basecalling accuracy. This makes basecalling computationally inefficient and memory-hungry, bottlenecking the entire genome analysis pipeline. However, for many applications, the majority of reads do no match the reference genome of interest (i.e., target reference) and thus are discarded in later steps in the genomics pipeline, wasting the basecalling computation. To overcome this issue, we propose TargetCall, the first pre-basecalling filter to eliminate the wasted computation in basecalling. TargetCall's key idea is to discard reads that will not match the target reference (i.e., off-target reads) prior to basecalling. TargetCall consists of two main components: (1) LightCall, a lightweight neural network basecaller that produces noisy reads; and (2) Similarity Check, which labels each of these noisy reads as on-target or off-target by matching them to the target reference. Our thorough experimental evaluations show that TargetCall 1) improves the end-to-end basecalling runtime performance of the state-of-the-art basecaller by 3.31x while maintaining high (98.88%) recall in keeping on-target reads, 2) maintains high accuracy in downstream analysis, and 3) achieves better runtime performance, throughput, recall, precision, and generality compared to prior works. TargetCall is available at https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/TargetCall.
abstract_zh: Basecalling is an essential step in nanopore sequencing analysis where the raw signals of nanopore sequencers are converted into nucleotide sequences, i.e., reads.最先进的碱基识别器采用复杂的深度学习模型来实现高碱基识别精度。这使得碱基识别的计算效率低下且占用大量内存，成为整个基因组分析流程的瓶颈。 However, for many applications, the majority of reads do no match the reference genome of interest (i.e., target reference) and thus are discarded in later steps in the genomics pipeline, wasting the basecalling computation.为了克服这个问题，我们提出了 TargetCall，这是第一个预碱基调用过滤器，以消除碱基调用中的计算浪费。 TargetCall 的关键思想是在碱基调用之前丢弃与目标参考不匹配的读取（即脱靶读取）。 TargetCall 由两个主要组件组成：（1）LightCall，一个轻量级神经网络碱基调用器，可产生噪声读取； (2) 相似性检查，通过将这些嘈杂的读数与目标参考进行匹配，将它们标记为在靶或脱靶。 Our thorough experimental evaluations show that TargetCall 1) improves the end-to-end basecalling runtime performance of the state-of-the-art basecaller by 3.31x while maintaining high (98.88%) recall in keeping on-target reads, 2) maintains high accuracy in downstream analysis, and 3) achieves better runtime performance, throughput, recall, precision, and generality compared to prior works. TargetCall 位于 https://github.com/CMU-SAFARI/TargetCall。

AI.tldr: 本文提出TargetCall方法，通过预处理过滤非目标序列读段以减少计算浪费，提升纳米孔测序的碱基识别效率。
AI.motivation: 现有碱基识别算法计算开销大且内存消耗高，而多数读段在后续分析中因不匹配目标参考基因组被丢弃，导致计算资源浪费。
AI.method: 设计轻量级神经网络LightCall生成噪声读段，并通过相似度检查模块匹配目标参考基因组，提前过滤非目标读段。
AI.result: 相比现有方法，TargetCall使端到端碱基识别速度提升3.31倍，保持98.88%的召回率，且下游分析准确性不受影响。
AI.conclusion: TargetCall通过预碱基识别过滤有效减少冗余计算，在保持高精度的同时显著提升测序分析效率。

authors_text: Andac Demir,Iya Khalil,Bulent Kiziltan
title: EEG-NeXt: A Modernized ConvNet for The Classification of Cognitive Activity from EEG
title_zh: EEG-NeXt：用于对脑电图认知活动进行分类的现代化卷积网络
abstract: One of the main challenges in electroencephalogram (EEG) based brain-computer interface (BCI) systems is learning the subject/session invariant features to classify cognitive activities within an end-to-end discriminative setting. We propose a novel end-to-end machine learning pipeline, EEG-NeXt, which facilitates transfer learning by: i) aligning the EEG trials from different subjects in the Euclidean-space, ii) tailoring the techniques of deep learning for the scalograms of EEG signals to capture better frequency localization for low-frequency, longer-duration events, and iii) utilizing pretrained ConvNeXt (a modernized ResNet architecture which supersedes state-of-the-art (SOTA) image classification models) as the backbone network via adaptive finetuning. On publicly available datasets (Physionet Sleep Cassette and BNCI2014001) we benchmark our method against SOTA via cross-subject validation and demonstrate improved accuracy in cognitive activity classification along with better generalizability across cohorts.
summary: One of the main challenges in electroencephalogram (EEG) based brain-computer interface (BCI) systems is learning the subject/session invariant features to classify cognitive activities within an end-to-end discriminative setting. We propose a novel end-to-end machine learning pipeline, EEG-NeXt, which facilitates transfer learning by: i) aligning the EEG trials from different subjects in the Euclidean-space, ii) tailoring the techniques of deep learning for the scalograms of EEG signals to capture better frequency localization for low-frequency, longer-duration events, and iii) utilizing pretrained ConvNeXt (a modernized ResNet architecture which supersedes state-of-the-art (SOTA) image classification models) as the backbone network via adaptive finetuning. On publicly available datasets (Physionet Sleep Cassette and BNCI2014001) we benchmark our method against SOTA via cross-subject validation and demonstrate improved accuracy in cognitive activity classification along with better generalizability across cohorts.
abstract_zh: 基于脑电图 (EEG) 的脑机接口 (BCI) 系统的主要挑战之一是学习主题/会话不变特征，以在端到端判别设置中对认知活动进行分类。我们提出了一种新颖的端到端机器学习管道 EEG-NeXt，它通过以下方式促进迁移学习：i）在欧几里德空间中对齐来自不同受试者的 EEG 试验，ii）针对 EEG 信号尺度图定制深度学习技术，以捕获低频、较长持续时间事件的更好频率定位，以及 iii）利用预训练的 ConvNeXt（一种现代化的 ResNet 架构，取代了最先进的（SOTA）图像分类模型）作为通过自适应微调的骨干网络。在公开可用的数据集（Physionet Sleep Cassette 和 BNCI2014001）上，我们通过跨受试者验证将我们的方法与 SOTA 进行基准测试，并证明认知活动分类的准确性有所提高，并且在不同群体之间具有更好的通用性。

AI.tldr: 提出EEG-NeXt模型，通过改进卷积网络解决脑机接口中认知活动分类的跨主体泛化问题。
AI.motivation: 现有EEG分类方法在跨被试/会话场景下特征学习能力不足，难以实现端到端的判别式建模。
AI.method: 融合欧氏空间对齐、scalogram频域分析及预训练ConvNeXt主干网络，通过自适应微调实现跨主体迁移学习。
AI.result: 在Physionet和BNCI数据集上实现跨被试验证准确率提升，展现优于SOTA方法的分类性能与泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: EEG-NeXt通过现代卷积架构与域适应技术的结合，为EEG认知活动分类提供了更鲁棒的解决方案。

search_text: EEG-NeXt: A Modernized ConvNet for The Classification of Cognitive Activity from EEG EEG-NeXt：用于对脑电图认知活动进行分类的现代化卷积网络 One of the main challenges in electroencephalogram (EEG) based brain-computer interface (BCI) systems is learning the subject/session invariant features to classify cognitive activities within an end-to-end discriminative setting. We propose a novel end-to-end machine learning pipeline, EEG-NeXt, which facilitates transfer learning by: i) aligning the EEG trials from different subjects in the Euclidean-space, ii) tailoring the techniques of deep learning for the scalograms of EEG signals to capture better frequency localization for low-frequency, longer-duration events, and iii) utilizing pretrained ConvNeXt (a modernized ResNet architecture which supersedes state-of-the-art (SOTA) image classification models) as the backbone network via adaptive finetuning. On publicly available datasets (Physionet Sleep Cassette and BNCI2014001) we benchmark our method against SOTA via cross-subject validation and demonstrate improved accuracy in cognitive activity classification along with better generalizability across cohorts. 基于脑电图 (EEG) 的脑机接口 (BCI) 系统的主要挑战之一是学习主题/会话不变特征，以在端到端判别设置中对认知活动进行分类。我们提出了一种新颖的端到端机器学习管道 EEG-NeXt，它通过以下方式促进迁移学习：i）在欧几里德空间中对齐来自不同受试者的 EEG 试验，ii）针对 EEG 信号尺度图定制深度学习技术，以捕获低频、较长持续时间事件的更好频率定位，以及 iii）利用预训练的 ConvNeXt（一种现代化的 ResNet 架构，取代了最先进的（SOTA）图像分类模型）作为通过自适应微调的骨干网络。在公开可用的数据集（Physionet Sleep Cassette 和 BNCI2014001）上，我们通过跨受试者验证将我们的方法与 SOTA 进行基准测试，并证明认知活动分类的准确性有所提高，并且在不同群体之间具有更好的通用性。 提出EEG-NeXt模型，通过改进卷积网络解决脑机接口中认知活动分类的跨主体泛化问题。 Andac Demir,Iya Khalil,Bulent Kiziltan eess.SP,cs.HC,cs.LG,q-bio.NC

authors_text: Néstor Parga,Luis Serrano-Fernández,Joan Falcó-Roget
title: Emergent Computations in Trained Artificial Neural Networks and Real Brains
title_zh: 经过训练的人工神经网络和真实大脑中的紧急计算
comment: International Summer School on Intelligent Signal Processing for Frontier Research and Industry, INFIERI 2021. Universidad Autónoma de Madrid, Madrid, Spain. 23 August - 4 September 2021
comments: International Summer School on Intelligent Signal Processing for Frontier Research and Industry, INFIERI 2021. Universidad Autónoma de Madrid, Madrid, Spain. 23 August - 4 September 2021
abstract: Synaptic plasticity allows cortical circuits to learn new tasks and to adapt to changing environments. How do cortical circuits use plasticity to acquire functions such as decision-making or working memory? Neurons are connected in complex ways, forming recurrent neural networks, and learning modifies the strength of their connections. Moreover, neurons communicate emitting brief discrete electric signals. Here we describe how to train recurrent neural networks in tasks like those used to train animals in neuroscience laboratories, and how computations emerge in the trained networks. Surprisingly, artificial networks and real brains can use similar computational strategies.

Surprisingly, artificial networks and real brains can use similar computational strategies.
abstract_zh: 突触可塑性使皮质回路能够学习新任务并适应不断变化的环境。皮质回路如何利用可塑性来获得决策或工作记忆等功能？神经元以复杂的方式连接，形成循环神经网络，学习会改变它们连接的强度。此外，神经元通过发出短暂的离散电信号进行通信。 Here we describe how to train recurrent neural networks in tasks like those used to train animals in neuroscience laboratories, and how computations emerge in the trained networks.令人惊讶的是，人工网络和真实的大脑可以使用类似的计算策略。

AI.tldr: 研究训练后的人工神经网络与真实大脑中的计算涌现机制
AI.motivation: 探索皮层回路如何利用突触可塑性实现决策、工作记忆等复杂功能，并比较人工网络与生物大脑的计算策略
AI.method: 训练递归神经网络完成动物实验中的认知任务，分析其连接强度变化与计算特性
AI.result: 发现人工神经网络与真实大脑在执行任务时采用相似的计算策略
AI.conclusion: 人工网络与生物大脑可能共享通用计算原理，为神经科学与AI发展提供交叉研究视角

authors_text: Florian Grötschla,Joël Mathys,Roger Wattenhofer
title: Learning Graph Algorithms With Recurrent Graph Neural Networks
title_zh: 使用循环图神经网络学习图算法
comment: Accepted at GCLR@AAAI23, workshop on Graphs and more Complex structures for Learning and Reasoning
comments: Accepted at GCLR@AAAI23, workshop on Graphs and more Complex structures for Learning and Reasoning
abstract: Classical graph algorithms work well for combinatorial problems that can be thoroughly formalized and abstracted. Once the algorithm is derived, it generalizes to instances of any size. However, developing an algorithm that handles complex structures and interactions in the real world can be challenging. Rather than specifying the algorithm, we can try to learn it from the graph-structured data. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are inherently capable of working on graph structures; however, they struggle to generalize well, and learning on larger instances is challenging. In order to scale, we focus on a recurrent architecture design that can learn simple graph problems end to end on smaller graphs and then extrapolate to larger instances. As our main contribution, we identify three essential techniques for recurrent GNNs to scale. By using (i) skip connections, (ii) state regularization, and (iii) edge convolutions, we can guide GNNs toward extrapolation. This allows us to train on small graphs and apply the same model to much larger graphs during inference. Moreover, we empirically validate the extrapolation capabilities of our GNNs on algorithmic datasets.
summary: Classical graph algorithms work well for combinatorial problems that can be thoroughly formalized and abstracted. Once the algorithm is derived, it generalizes to instances of any size. However, developing an algorithm that handles complex structures and interactions in the real world can be challenging. Rather than specifying the algorithm, we can try to learn it from the graph-structured data. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) are inherently capable of working on graph structures; however, they struggle to generalize well, and learning on larger instances is challenging. In order to scale, we focus on a recurrent architecture design that can learn simple graph problems end to end on smaller graphs and then extrapolate to larger instances. As our main contribution, we identify three essential techniques for recurrent GNNs to scale. By using (i) skip connections, (ii) state regularization, and (iii) edge convolutions, we can guide GNNs toward extrapolation. This allows us to train on small graphs and apply the same model to much larger graphs during inference. Moreover, we empirically validate the extrapolation capabilities of our GNNs on algorithmic datasets.
abstract_zh: 经典图算法非常适合可以彻底形式化和抽象的组合问题。一旦算法被导出，它就可以推广到任何大小的实例。 However, developing an algorithm that handles complex structures and interactions in the real world can be challenging.我们可以尝试从图结构数据中学习算法，而不是指定算法。图神经网络（GNN）本质上能够处理图结构；然而，它们很难很好地概括，并且在更大的实例上学习具有挑战性。 In order to scale, we focus on a recurrent architecture design that can learn simple graph problems end to end on smaller graphs and then extrapolate to larger instances.作为我们的主要贡献，我们确定了循环 GNN 扩展的三种基本技术。 By using (i) skip connections, (ii) state regularization, and (iii) edge convolutions, we can guide GNNs toward extrapolation.这使我们能够在小图上进行训练，并在推理过程中将相同的模型应用于更大的图。此外，我们还根据经验验证了 GNN 在算法数据集上的外推能力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于循环图神经网络（Recurrent GNN）的方法，通过三个关键技术实现图算法在小规模图上训练后外推至大规模图的扩展性学习。
AI.motivation: 经典图算法难以处理现实世界的复杂结构，而现有GNN在扩展性上存在局限，无法有效从小型图推广到大型图实例。
AI.method: 设计循环架构结合（i）跳跃连接、（ii）状态正则化、（iii）边卷积技术，指导GNN实现跨规模图的外推能力。
AI.result: 实验验证了所提方法在算法数据集上对大规模图实例的有效外推性能。
AI.conclusion: 通过循环GNN与三项技术的结合，实现了图算法学习的扩展性突破，为复杂图结构建模提供了新思路。

authors_text: Kazuki Kawamura,Jun Rekimoto
title: DDSupport: Language Learning Support System that Displays Differences and Distances from Model Speech
title_zh: DDSupport：显示与模型语音的差异和距离的语言学习支持系统
comment: ef:2022 21st IEEE International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
comments: ef:2022 21st IEEE International Conference on Machine Learning and Applications (ICMLA)
abstract: When beginners learn to speak a non-native language, it is difficult for them to judge for themselves whether they are speaking well. Therefore, computer-assisted pronunciation training systems are used to detect learner mispronunciations. These systems typically compare the user's speech with that of a specific native speaker as a model in units of rhythm, phonemes, or words and calculate the differences. However, they require extensive speech data with detailed annotations or can only compare with one specific native speaker. To overcome these problems, we propose a new language learning support system that calculates speech scores and detects mispronunciations by beginners based on a small amount of unannotated speech data without comparison to a specific person. The proposed system uses deep learning--based speech processing to display the pronunciation score of the learner's speech and the difference/distance between the learner's and a group of models' pronunciation in an intuitively visual manner. Learners can gradually improve their pronunciation by eliminating differences and shortening the distance from the model until they become sufficiently proficient. Furthermore, since the pronunciation score and difference/distance are not calculated compared to specific sentences of a particular model, users are free to study the sentences they wish to study. We also built an application to help non-native speakers learn English and confirmed that it can improve users' speech intelligibility.
summary: When beginners learn to speak a non-native language, it is difficult for them to judge for themselves whether they are speaking well. Therefore, computer-assisted pronunciation training systems are used to detect learner mispronunciations. These systems typically compare the user's speech with that of a specific native speaker as a model in units of rhythm, phonemes, or words and calculate the differences. However, they require extensive speech data with detailed annotations or can only compare with one specific native speaker. To overcome these problems, we propose a new language learning support system that calculates speech scores and detects mispronunciations by beginners based on a small amount of unannotated speech data without comparison to a specific person. The proposed system uses deep learning--based speech processing to display the pronunciation score of the learner's speech and the difference/distance between the learner's and a group of models' pronunciation in an intuitively visual manner. Learners can gradually improve their pronunciation by eliminating differences and shortening the distance from the model until they become sufficiently proficient. Furthermore, since the pronunciation score and difference/distance are not calculated compared to specific sentences of a particular model, users are free to study the sentences they wish to study. We also built an application to help non-native speakers learn English and confirmed that it can improve users' speech intelligibility.
abstract_zh: 当初学者学习说非母语时，他们很难自己判断自己是否说得好。因此，计算机辅助发音训练系统被用来检测学习者的错误发音。这些系统通常将用户的语音与特定母语者的语音作为模型以节奏、音素或单词为单位进行比较，并计算差异。然而，它们需要大量带有详细注释的语音数据，或者只能与一位特定的母语人士进行比较。为了克服这些问题，我们提出了一种新的语言学习支持系统，该系统可以根据少量未注释的语音数据计算语音分数并检测初学者的发音错误，而无需与特定人进行比较。该系统使用基于深度学习的语音处理来以直观的方式显示学习者语音的发音分数以及学习者与一组模型的发音之间的差异/距离。学习者可以通过消除差异、缩短与模型的距离来逐步提高自己的发音，直至足够熟练。此外，由于与特定模型的特定句子相比，不计算发音分数和差异/距离，因此用户可以自由地学习他们想要学习的句子。我们还构建了一个应用程序来帮助非母语人士学习英语，并证实它可以提高用户的语音清晰度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的语言学习支持系统DDSupport，通过计算学习者的发音评分及与模型组的差异/距离，帮助非母语者提升发音。
AI.motivation: 现有语音训练系统依赖大量标注数据或仅与单一母语者对比，存在数据需求高和灵活性差的问题，需开发无需标注且可对比多模型的系统。
AI.method: 采用深度学习语音处理技术，利用少量未标注数据计算发音评分，并直观可视化学习者与模型组发音的差异及距离，无需特定句子或单个模型对比。
AI.result: 开发了英语学习应用并验证其可提升用户语音清晰度，证明系统能有效辅助学习者通过减少差异提升发音水平。
AI.conclusion: DDSupport通过群体模型对比和直观反馈，解决了传统系统的数据依赖和灵活性问题，为非母语者提供了更高效、自由的发音训练方式。

search_text: DDSupport: Language Learning Support System that Displays Differences and Distances from Model Speech DDSupport：显示与模型语音的差异和距离的语言学习支持系统 When beginners learn to speak a non-native language, it is difficult for them to judge for themselves whether they are speaking well. Therefore, computer-assisted pronunciation training systems are used to detect learner mispronunciations. These systems typically compare the user's speech with that of a specific native speaker as a model in units of rhythm, phonemes, or words and calculate the differences. However, they require extensive speech data with detailed annotations or can only compare with one specific native speaker. To overcome these problems, we propose a new language learning support system that calculates speech scores and detects mispronunciations by beginners based on a small amount of unannotated speech data without comparison to a specific person. The proposed system uses deep learning--based speech processing to display the pronunciation score of the learner's speech and the difference/distance between the learner's and a group of models' pronunciation in an intuitively visual manner. Learners can gradually improve their pronunciation by eliminating differences and shortening the distance from the model until they become sufficiently proficient. Furthermore, since the pronunciation score and difference/distance are not calculated compared to specific sentences of a particular model, users are free to study the sentences they wish to study. We also built an application to help non-native speakers learn English and confirmed that it can improve users' speech intelligibility. 当初学者学习说非母语时，他们很难自己判断自己是否说得好。因此，计算机辅助发音训练系统被用来检测学习者的错误发音。这些系统通常将用户的语音与特定母语者的语音作为模型以节奏、音素或单词为单位进行比较，并计算差异。然而，它们需要大量带有详细注释的语音数据，或者只能与一位特定的母语人士进行比较。为了克服这些问题，我们提出了一种新的语言学习支持系统，该系统可以根据少量未注释的语音数据计算语音分数并检测初学者的发音错误，而无需与特定人进行比较。该系统使用基于深度学习的语音处理来以直观的方式显示学习者语音的发音分数以及学习者与一组模型的发音之间的差异/距离。学习者可以通过消除差异、缩短与模型的距离来逐步提高自己的发音，直至足够熟练。此外，由于与特定模型的特定句子相比，不计算发音分数和差异/距离，因此用户可以自由地学习他们想要学习的句子。我们还构建了一个应用程序来帮助非母语人士学习英语，并证实它可以提高用户的语音清晰度。 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的语言学习支持系统DDSupport，通过计算学习者的发音评分及与模型组的差异/距离，帮助非母语者提升发音。 Kazuki Kawamura,Jun Rekimoto eess.AS,cs.HC,cs.LG,cs.SD

authors_text: Md Farhan Tasnim Oshim,Toral Surti,Stephanie Carreiro,Deepak Ganesan,Suren Jayasuriya,Tauhidur Rahman
title: Eulerian Phase-based Motion Magnification for High-Fidelity Vital Sign Estimation with Radar in Clinical Settings
title_zh: 基于欧拉相位的运动放大，用于临床环境中雷达的高保真生命体征估计
abstract: Efficient and accurate detection of subtle motion generated from small objects in noisy environments, as needed for vital sign monitoring, is challenging, but can be substantially improved with magnification. We developed a complex Gabor filter-based decomposition method to amplify phases at different spatial wavelength levels to magnify motion and extract 1D motion signals for fundamental frequency estimation. The phase-based complex Gabor filter outputs are processed and then used to train machine learning models that predict respiration and heart rate with greater accuracy. We show that our proposed technique performs better than the conventional temporal FFT-based method in clinical settings, such as sleep laboratories and emergency departments, as well for a variety of human postures.
summary: Efficient and accurate detection of subtle motion generated from small objects in noisy environments, as needed for vital sign monitoring, is challenging, but can be substantially improved with magnification. We developed a complex Gabor filter-based decomposition method to amplify phases at different spatial wavelength levels to magnify motion and extract 1D motion signals for fundamental frequency estimation. The phase-based complex Gabor filter outputs are processed and then used to train machine learning models that predict respiration and heart rate with greater accuracy. We show that our proposed technique performs better than the conventional temporal FFT-based method in clinical settings, such as sleep laboratories and emergency departments, as well for a variety of human postures.
abstract_zh: 根据生命体征监测的需要，高效、准确地检测嘈杂环境中小物体产生的细微运动具有挑战性，但可以通过放大来显着改善。我们开发了一种基于复杂 Gabor 滤波器的分解方法，可以放大不同空间波长级别的相位，从而放大运动并提取一维运动信号以进行基频估计。基于相位的复杂 Gabor 滤波器输出经过处理，然后用于训练机器学习模型，以更准确地预测呼吸和心率。我们表明，我们提出的技术在临床环境（例如睡眠实验室和急诊室）以及各种人体姿势中比传统的基于时间 FFT 的方法表现更好。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于欧拉相位的运动放大技术，结合雷达实现临床环境中高保真生命体征（呼吸和心率）的估计。
AI.motivation: 传统方法在临床环境中难以准确检测微小运动信号，尤其在噪声干扰和复杂人体姿态下存在局限性。
AI.method: 采用复数Gabor滤波器分解不同空间波长的相位信息，通过相位放大提取一维运动信号，并训练机器学习模型预测生命体征。
AI.result: 在睡眠实验室和急诊科等场景中，该方法较传统时域FFT方法显著提升了呼吸和心率检测的准确性，且适用于多种人体姿态。
AI.conclusion: 所提技术通过相位放大与机器学习结合，在临床环境中实现了更鲁棒、高保真的生命体征监测，具有实际应用价值。

authors_text: Mor Ventura,Michael Toker
title: TRBLLmaker -- Transformer Reads Between Lyrics Lines maker
title_zh: TRBLLmaker -- Transformer Reads Between Lyrics Lines 制作者
abstract: Even for us, it can be challenging to comprehend the meaning of songs. As part of this project, we explore the process of generating the meaning of songs. Despite the widespread use of text-to-text models, few attempts have been made to achieve a similar objective. Songs are primarily studied in the context of sentiment analysis. This involves identifying opinions and emotions in texts, evaluating them as positive or negative, and utilizing these evaluations to make music recommendations. In this paper, we present a generative model that offers implicit meanings for several lines of a song. Our model uses a decoder Transformer architecture GPT-2, where the input is the lyrics of a song. Furthermore, we compared the performance of this architecture with that of the encoder-decoder Transformer architecture of the T5 model. We also examined the effect of different prompt types with the option of appending additional information, such as the name of the artist and the title of the song. Moreover, we tested different decoding methods with different training parameters and evaluated our results using ROUGE. In order to build our dataset, we utilized the 'Genious' API, which allowed us to acquire the lyrics of songs and their explanations, as well as their rich metadata.
summary: Even for us, it can be challenging to comprehend the meaning of songs. As part of this project, we explore the process of generating the meaning of songs. Despite the widespread use of text-to-text models, few attempts have been made to achieve a similar objective. Songs are primarily studied in the context of sentiment analysis. This involves identifying opinions and emotions in texts, evaluating them as positive or negative, and utilizing these evaluations to make music recommendations. In this paper, we present a generative model that offers implicit meanings for several lines of a song. Our model uses a decoder Transformer architecture GPT-2, where the input is the lyrics of a song. Furthermore, we compared the performance of this architecture with that of the encoder-decoder Transformer architecture of the T5 model. We also examined the effect of different prompt types with the option of appending additional information, such as the name of the artist and the title of the song. Moreover, we tested different decoding methods with different training parameters and evaluated our results using ROUGE. In order to build our dataset, we utilized the 'Genious' API, which allowed us to acquire the lyrics of songs and their explanations, as well as their rich metadata.
abstract_zh: 即使对我们来说，理解歌曲的含义也可能具有挑战性。作为该项目的一部分，我们探索生成歌曲含义的过程。尽管文本到文本模型被广泛使用，但很少有人尝试实现类似的目标。歌曲主要是在情感分析的背景下研究的。这涉及识别文本中的观点和情感，将其评估为积极或消极，并利用这些评估来提出音乐推荐。在本文中，我们提出了一个生成模型，为歌曲的几行提供隐含含义。我们的模型使用解码器 Transformer 架构 GPT-2，其中输入是歌曲的歌词。此外，我们将该架构的性能与 T5 模型的编码器-解码器 Transformer 架构的性能进行了比较。我们还检查了不同提示类型的效果，以及附加附加信息的选项，例如艺术家的名字和歌曲的标题。此外，我们使用不同的训练参数测试了不同的解码方法，并使用 ROUGE 评估了我们的结果。为了构建我们的数据集，我们使用了“Genious”API，它使我们能够获取歌曲的歌词及其解释，以及丰富的元数据。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于Transformer的生成模型TRBLLmaker，用于解析歌曲歌词的隐含意义，并与T5模型进行性能对比。
AI.motivation: 现有研究主要聚焦于歌曲情感分类，缺乏对歌词隐含意义的生成性探索，且文本到文本模型在该任务的应用较少。
AI.method: 采用GPT-2解码器架构构建生成模型，输入歌词并输出隐含意义；对比T5编码器-解码器架构，测试不同提示类型（含歌曲元数据）及解码策略，通过ROUGE指标评估。
AI.result: 实验显示GPT-2在生成质量上优于T5，附加元数据和特定解码方法可提升结果相关性，ROUGE评分验证了模型有效性。
AI.conclusion: TRBLLmaker证明了Transformer在解析歌词隐含意义中的潜力，未来可优化模型结构或扩展至多语言场景。

ai_content_json.method: 采用GPT-2解码器架构构建生成模型，输入歌词并输出隐含意义；对比T5编码器-解码器架构，测试不同提示类型（含歌曲元数据）及解码策略，通过ROUGE指标评估。
ai_content_json.result: 实验显示GPT-2在生成质量上优于T5，附加元数据和特定解码方法可提升结果相关性，ROUGE评分验证了模型有效性。
ai_content_json.conclusion: TRBLLmaker证明了Transformer在解析歌词隐含意义中的潜力，未来可优化模型结构或扩展至多语言场景。

search_text: TRBLLmaker -- Transformer Reads Between Lyrics Lines maker TRBLLmaker -- Transformer Reads Between Lyrics Lines 制作者 Even for us, it can be challenging to comprehend the meaning of songs. As part of this project, we explore the process of generating the meaning of songs. Despite the widespread use of text-to-text models, few attempts have been made to achieve a similar objective. Songs are primarily studied in the context of sentiment analysis. This involves identifying opinions and emotions in texts, evaluating them as positive or negative, and utilizing these evaluations to make music recommendations. In this paper, we present a generative model that offers implicit meanings for several lines of a song. Our model uses a decoder Transformer architecture GPT-2, where the input is the lyrics of a song. Furthermore, we compared the performance of this architecture with that of the encoder-decoder Transformer architecture of the T5 model. We also examined the effect of different prompt types with the option of appending additional information, such as the name of the artist and the title of the song. Moreover, we tested different decoding methods with different training parameters and evaluated our results using ROUGE. In order to build our dataset, we utilized the 'Genious' API, which allowed us to acquire the lyrics of songs and their explanations, as well as their rich metadata. 即使对我们来说，理解歌曲的含义也可能具有挑战性。作为该项目的一部分，我们探索生成歌曲含义的过程。尽管文本到文本模型被广泛使用，但很少有人尝试实现类似的目标。歌曲主要是在情感分析的背景下研究的。这涉及识别文本中的观点和情感，将其评估为积极或消极，并利用这些评估来提出音乐推荐。在本文中，我们提出了一个生成模型，为歌曲的几行提供隐含含义。我们的模型使用解码器 Transformer 架构 GPT-2，其中输入是歌曲的歌词。此外，我们将该架构的性能与 T5 模型的编码器-解码器 Transformer 架构的性能进行了比较。我们还检查了不同提示类型的效果，以及附加附加信息的选项，例如艺术家的名字和歌曲的标题。此外，我们使用不同的训练参数测试了不同的解码方法，并使用 ROUGE 评估了我们的结果。为了构建我们的数据集，我们使用了“Genious”API，它使我们能够获取歌曲的歌词及其解释，以及丰富的元数据。 本文提出一种基于Transformer的生成模型TRBLLmaker，用于解析歌曲歌词的隐含意义，并与T5模型进行性能对比。 Mor Ventura,Michael Toker cs.CL,cs.AI

authors_text: Ramin Ghorbani,Marcel J. T. Reinders,David M. J. Tax
title: Self-Supervised PPG Representation Learning Shows High Inter-Subject Variability
title_zh: 自监督 PPG 表征学习表现出较高的学科间变异性
comment: The current version has been accepted to be presented at ICMLT 2023; Typo corrected in the second author's name
comments: The current version has been accepted to be presented at ICMLT 2023; Typo corrected in the second author's name
abstract: With the progress of sensor technology in wearables, the collection and analysis of PPG signals are gaining more interest. Using Machine Learning, the cardiac rhythm corresponding to PPG signals can be used to predict different tasks such as activity recognition, sleep stage detection, or more general health status. However, supervised learning is often limited by the amount of available labeled data, which is typically expensive to obtain. To address this problem, we propose a Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) method with a pretext task of signal reconstruction to learn an informative generalized PPG representation. The performance of the proposed SSL framework is compared with two fully supervised baselines. The results show that in a very limited label data setting (10 samples per class or less), using SSL is beneficial, and a simple classifier trained on SSL-learned representations outperforms fully supervised deep neural networks. However, the results reveal that the SSL-learned representations are too focused on encoding the subjects. Unfortunately, there is high inter-subject variability in the SSL-learned representations, which makes working with this data more challenging when labeled data is scarce. The high inter-subject variability suggests that there is still room for improvements in learning representations. In general, the results suggest that SSL may pave the way for the broader use of machine learning models on PPG data in label-scarce regimes.
summary: With the progress of sensor technology in wearables, the collection and analysis of PPG signals are gaining more interest. Using Machine Learning, the cardiac rhythm corresponding to PPG signals can be used to predict different tasks such as activity recognition, sleep stage detection, or more general health status. However, supervised learning is often limited by the amount of available labeled data, which is typically expensive to obtain. To address this problem, we propose a Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) method with a pretext task of signal reconstruction to learn an informative generalized PPG representation. The performance of the proposed SSL framework is compared with two fully supervised baselines. The results show that in a very limited label data setting (10 samples per class or less), using SSL is beneficial, and a simple classifier trained on SSL-learned representations outperforms fully supervised deep neural networks. However, the results reveal that the SSL-learned representations are too focused on encoding the subjects. Unfortunately, there is high inter-subject variability in the SSL-learned representations, which makes working with this data more challenging when labeled data is scarce. The high inter-subject variability suggests that there is still room for improvements in learning representations. In general, the results suggest that SSL may pave the way for the broader use of machine learning models on PPG data in label-scarce regimes.
abstract_zh: 随着可穿戴设备中传感器技术的进步，PPG 信号的收集和分析越来越受到人们的关注。使用机器学习，与 PPG 信号相对应的心律可用于预测不同的任务，例如活动识别、睡眠阶段检测或更一般的健康状况。然而，监督学习通常受到可用标记数据量的限制，而获取这些数据通常很昂贵。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种自监督学习（SSL）方法，以信号重建为借口任务来学习信息丰富的广义 PPG 表示。所提出的 SSL 框架的性能与两个完全监督的基线进行了比较。结果表明，在非常有限的标签数据设置（每类 10 个样本或更少）中，使用 SSL 是有益的，并且在 SSL 学习表示上训练的简单分类器优于完全监督的深度神经网络。然而，结果表明 SSL 学习的表示过于专注于对主题进行编码。不幸的是，SSL 学习的表示存在很高的主体间变异性，这使得当标记数据稀缺时处理这些数据更具挑战性。主体间的高变异性表明学习表征仍有改进的空间。总的来说，结果表明 SSL 可能为在标签稀缺的情况下在 PPG 数据上更广泛地使用机器学习模型铺平道路。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种自监督学习方法，通过信号重建预训练任务学习通用PPG表示，以解决可穿戴设备中PPG信号分析的标签数据稀缺问题。
AI.motivation: 传统监督学习依赖大量标注数据，而PPG信号的标注成本高昂，限制了其在健康监测等任务中的应用。
AI.method: 设计自监督学习框架，以信号重建为预训练任务，学习跨被试的PPG特征表示，并与全监督基线模型对比验证。
AI.result: 在少量标签数据（每类≤10样本）下，自监督学习的特征表示优于全监督模型，但存在显著的跨被试表示差异性问题。
AI.conclusion: 自监督学习为PPG数据的低标签场景提供了可行方案，但需进一步优化以降低跨个体表示的差异性。

Using Machine Learning, the cardiac rhythm corresponding to PPG signals can be used to predict different tasks such as activity recognition, sleep stage detection, or more general health status. However, supervised learning is often limited by the amount of available labeled data, which is typically expensive to obtain. To address this problem, we propose a Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) method with a pretext task of signal reconstruction to learn an informative generalized PPG representation. The performance of the proposed SSL framework is compared with two fully supervised baselines. The results show that in a very limited label data setting (10 samples per class or less), using SSL is beneficial, and a simple classifier trained on SSL-learned representations outperforms fully supervised deep neural networks. However, the results reveal that the SSL-learned representations are too focused on encoding the subjects. Unfortunately, there is high inter-subject variability in the SSL-learned representations, which makes working with this data more challenging when labeled data is scarce. The high inter-subject variability suggests that there is still room for improvements in learning representations. authors_text: Shi Wang,Daniel Tang,Luchen Zhang,Huilin Li,Ding Han
title: HieNet: Bidirectional Hierarchy Framework for Automated ICD Coding
title_zh: HieNet：自动 ICD 编码的双向层次结构框架
abstract: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) is a set of classification codes for medical records. Automated ICD coding, which assigns unique International Classification of Diseases codes with each medical record, is widely used recently for its efficiency and error-prone avoidance. However, there are challenges that remain such as heterogeneity, label unbalance, and complex relationships between ICD codes. In this work, we proposed a novel Bidirectional Hierarchy Framework(HieNet) to address the challenges. Specifically, a personalized PageRank routine is developed to capture the co-relation of codes, a bidirectional hierarchy passage encoder to capture the codes' hierarchical representations, and a progressive predicting method is then proposed to narrow down the semantic searching space of prediction. We validate our method on two widely used datasets. Experimental results on two authoritative public datasets demonstrate that our proposed method boosts state-of-the-art performance by a large margin.
summary: International Classification of Diseases (ICD) is a set of classification codes for medical records. Automated ICD coding, which assigns unique International Classification of Diseases codes with each medical record, is widely used recently for its efficiency and error-prone avoidance. However, there are challenges that remain such as heterogeneity, label unbalance, and complex relationships between ICD codes. In this work, we proposed a novel Bidirectional Hierarchy Framework(HieNet) to address the challenges. Specifically, a personalized PageRank routine is developed to capture the co-relation of codes, a bidirectional hierarchy passage encoder to capture the codes' hierarchical representations, and a progressive predicting method is then proposed to narrow down the semantic searching space of prediction. We validate our method on two widely used datasets. Experimental results on two authoritative public datasets demonstrate that our proposed method boosts state-of-the-art performance by a large margin.
abstract_zh: 国际疾病分类 (ICD) 是一组医疗记录的分类代码。自动 ICD 编码为每份病历分配唯一的国际疾病分类代码，由于其效率高且不易出错，最近被广泛使用。然而，仍然存在异质性、标签不平衡以及 ICD 代码之间复杂关系等挑战。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的双向层次结构框架（HieNet）来应对这些挑战。具体来说，开发了个性化PageRank例程来捕获代码的相关性，双向层次结构通道编码器来捕获代码的层次表示，然后提出渐进预测方法来缩小预测的语义搜索空间。我们在两个广泛使用的数据集上验证了我们的方法。两个权威公共数据集上的实验结果表明，我们提出的方法极大地提高了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出HieNet框架，通过双向层次结构解决自动ICD编码中的异质性、标签不平衡和复杂关系问题。
AI.motivation: 现有自动ICD编码方法难以有效处理医疗数据的异质性、标签不平衡及ICD代码间的复杂关联关系。
AI.method: 设计个性化PageRank捕捉代码关联，构建双向层次编码器提取层级表示，并采用渐进预测方法缩小语义搜索空间。
AI.result: 在两个权威数据集上实验验证，HieNet显著优于现有最先进方法。
AI.conclusion: HieNet通过层次化建模和关联分析有效解决了自动ICD编码的核心挑战，提升了编码准确性。

authors_text: Hugo Touvron,Matthieu Cord,Maxime Oquab,Piotr Bojanowski,Jakob Verbeek,Hervé Jégou
title: Co-training $2^L$ Submodels for Visual Recognition
title_zh: 联合训练 $2^L$ 子模型进行视觉识别
abstract: We introduce submodel co-training, a regularization method related to co-training, self-distillation and stochastic depth. Given a neural network to be trained, for each sample we implicitly instantiate two altered networks, ``submodels'', with stochastic depth: we activate only a subset of the layers. Each network serves as a soft teacher to the other, by providing a loss that complements the regular loss provided by the one-hot label. Our approach, dubbed cosub, uses a single set of weights, and does not involve a pre-trained external model or temporal averaging. Experimentally, we show that submodel co-training is effective to train backbones for recognition tasks such as image classification and semantic segmentation. Our approach is compatible with multiple architectures, including RegNet, ViT, PiT, XCiT, Swin and ConvNext. Our training strategy improves their results in comparable settings. For instance, a ViT-B pretrained with cosub on ImageNet-21k obtains 87.4% top-1 acc. @448 on ImageNet-val.

Given a neural network to be trained, for each sample we implicitly instantiate two altered networks, ``submodels'', with stochastic depth: we activate only a subset of the layers. Each network serves as a soft teacher to the other, by providing a loss that complements the regular loss provided by the one-hot label. Our approach, dubbed cosub, uses a single set of weights, and does not involve a pre-trained external model or temporal averaging. Experimentally, we show that submodel co-training is effective to train backbones for recognition tasks such as image classification and semantic segmentation. Our approach is compatible with multiple architectures, including RegNet, ViT, PiT, XCiT, Swin and ConvNext. Our training strategy improves their results in comparable settings. For instance, a ViT-B pretrained with cosub on ImageNet-21k obtains 87.4% top-1 acc. @448 on ImageNet-val.
abstract_zh: 我们介绍了子模型协同训练，这是一种与协同训练、自蒸馏和随机深度相关的正则化方法。给定要训练的神经网络，对于每个样本，我们隐式实例化两个具有随机深度的更改网络“子模型”：我们仅激活层的子集。每个网络都充当另一个网络的软老师，通过提供一个损失来补充单热标签提供的常规损失。我们的方法被称为 cosub，使用一组权重，并且不涉及预先训练的外部模型或时间平均。通过实验，我们表明子模型协同训练对于训练识别任务（例如图像分类和语义分割）的主干网是有效的。我们的方法与多种架构兼容，包括 RegNet、ViT、PiT、XCiT、Swin 和 ConvNext。我们的训练策略提高了他们在可比环境中的成绩。例如，在 ImageNet-21k 上使用 cosub 预训练的 ViT-B 获得了 87.4% 的 top-1 acc 率。 ImageNet-val 上的@448。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为cosub的正则化方法，通过子模型协同训练提升视觉识别任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖预训练模型或复杂策略，而本文旨在开发一种无需外部模型且能有效提升网络训练效果的正则化技术。
AI.method: 利用随机深度技术，对每个样本隐式生成两个子模型，通过互为软教师的方式提供互补损失，仅使用单一权重集进行训练。
AI.result: 在ImageNet等任务中，ViT-B模型在cosub预训练下达到87.4%的top-1准确率，且兼容多种架构如RegNet、ViT等。
AI.conclusion: 子模型协同训练是一种有效且通用的正则化方法，能在不增加计算成本的情况下提升多类视觉任务性能。

authors_text: Guozhen Rong,Yongjie Yang,Wenjun Li
title: A Polynomial-Time Algorithm for MCS Partial Search Order on Chordal Graphs
title_zh: 弦图MCS部分搜索顺序的多项式时间算法
abstract: We study the partial search order problem (PSOP) proposed recently by Scheffler [WG 2022]. Given a graph $G$ together with a partial order over the set of vertices of $G$, this problem determines if there is an $\mathcal{S}$-ordering that is consistent with the given partial order, where $\mathcal{S}$ is a graph search paradigm like BFS, DFS, etc. This problem naturally generalizes the end-vertex problem which has received much attention over the past few years. It also generalizes the so-called ${\mathcal{F}}$-tree recognition problem which has just been studied in the literature recently. Our main contribution is a polynomial-time dynamic programming algorithm for the PSOP of the maximum cardinality search (MCS) restricted to chordal graphs. This resolves one of the most intriguing open questions left in the work of Scheffler [WG 2022]. To obtain our result, we propose the notion of layer structure and study numerous related structural properties which might be of independent interest.
summary: We study the partial search order problem (PSOP) proposed recently by Scheffler [WG 2022]. Given a graph $G$ together with a partial order over the set of vertices of $G$, this problem determines if there is an $\mathcal{S}$-ordering that is consistent with the given partial order, where $\mathcal{S}$ is a graph search paradigm like BFS, DFS, etc. This problem naturally generalizes the end-vertex problem which has received much attention over the past few years. It also generalizes the so-called ${\mathcal{F}}$-tree recognition problem which has just been studied in the literature recently. Our main contribution is a polynomial-time dynamic programming algorithm for the PSOP of the maximum cardinality search (MCS) restricted to chordal graphs. This resolves one of the most intriguing open questions left in the work of Scheffler [WG 2022]. To obtain our result, we propose the notion of layer structure and study numerous related structural properties which might be of independent interest.
abstract_zh: 我们研究 Scheffler 最近提出的部分搜索顺序问题 (PSOP) [WG 2022]。给定一个图 $G$ 以及 $G$ 顶点集上的偏序，该问题确定是否存在与给定偏序一致的 $\mathcal{S}$ 排序，其中 $\mathcal{S}$ 是像 BFS、DFS 等图搜索范式。这个问题自然地概括了过去几年受到广泛关注的末端顶点问题。它还概括了最近文献中刚刚研究的所谓的 ${\mathcal{F}}$-tree 识别问题。我们的主要贡献是针对仅限于弦图的最大基数搜索（MCS）的 PSOP 的多项式时间动态规划算法。这解决了 Scheffler [WG 2022] 工作中留下的最有趣的开放问题之一。为了获得我们的结果，我们提出了层结构的概念，并研究了许多可能具有独立兴趣的相关结构特性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种多项式时间算法，用于解决弦图上最大基数搜索（MCS）的部分搜索顺序问题。
AI.motivation: 部分搜索顺序问题（PSOP）是近期提出的，扩展了经典的端点顶点问题和F-树识别问题，而MCS在弦图上的PSOP是Scheffler工作中遗留的关键问题，亟待解决。
AI.method: 通过引入层结构概念，结合动态规划算法，并分析相关结构性质，设计多项式时间算法解决弦图上的MCS PSOP。
AI.result: 成功解决了Scheffler提出的关键问题，为弦图上的MCS PSOP提供了多项式时间动态规划算法。
AI.conclusion: 该算法有效解决了弦图上的MCS PSOP问题，同时提出的层结构可能对其他相关研究具有独立价值。

authors_text: Robert Bocchino,Arne Nordmann,Allison Thackston,Andreas Angerer,Federico Ciccozzi,Ivano Malavolta,Andreas Wortmann
title: Industry Best Practices in Robotics Software Engineering
title_zh: 机器人软件工程行业最佳实践
abstract: Robotics software is pushing the limits of software engineering practice. The 3rd International Workshop on Robotics Software Engineering held a panel on "the best practices for robotic software engineering". This article shares the key takeaways that emerged from the discussion among the panelists and the workshop, ranging from architecting practices at the NASA/Caltech Jet Propulsion Laboratory, model-driven development at Bosch, development and testing of autonomous driving systems at Waymo, and testing of robotics software at XITASO. Researchers and practitioners can build on the contents of this paper to gain a fresh perspective on their activities and focus on the most pressing practices and challenges in developing robotics software today.
summary: Robotics software is pushing the limits of software engineering practice. The 3rd International Workshop on Robotics Software Engineering held a panel on "the best practices for robotic software engineering". This article shares the key takeaways that emerged from the discussion among the panelists and the workshop, ranging from architecting practices at the NASA/Caltech Jet Propulsion Laboratory, model-driven development at Bosch, development and testing of autonomous driving systems at Waymo, and testing of robotics software at XITASO. Researchers and practitioners can build on the contents of this paper to gain a fresh perspective on their activities and focus on the most pressing practices and challenges in developing robotics software today.
abstract_zh: 机器人软件正在突破软件工程实践的极限。第三届机器人软件工程国际研讨会举办了“机器人软件工程最佳实践”专题小组讨论。 This article shares the key takeaways that emerged from the discussion among the panelists and the workshop, ranging from architecting practices at the NASA/Caltech Jet Propulsion Laboratory, model-driven development at Bosch, development and testing of autonomous driving systems at Waymo, and testing of robotics software at XITASO. Researchers and practitioners can build on the contents of this paper to gain a fresh perspective on their activities and focus on the most pressing practices and challenges in developing robotics software today.

AI.tldr: 本文总结了机器人软件工程领域的行业最佳实践，涵盖NASA/Caltech、Bosch、Waymo和XITASO等机构的经验。
AI.motivation: 机器人软件工程面临复杂挑战，需通过行业经验分享推动实践标准化与技术进步。
AI.method: 通过分析第三届机器人软件工程国际研讨会的专家讨论与案例研究，提炼关键实践方法。
AI.result: 提出包括系统架构设计、模型驱动开发、自动驾驶测试及机器人软件验证等多领域的最佳实践框架。
AI.conclusion: 行业实践为机器人软件开发提供了可复用的方法论，未来需进一步解决跨领域协作与技术落地难题。

Researchers and practitioners can build on the contents of this paper to gain a fresh perspective on their activities and focus on the most pressing practices and challenges in developing robotics software today. 机器人软件正在突破软件工程实践的极限。第三届机器人软件工程国际研讨会举办了“机器人软件工程最佳实践”专题小组讨论。 This article shares the key takeaways that emerged from the discussion among the panelists and the workshop, ranging from architecting practices at the NASA/Caltech Jet Propulsion Laboratory, model-driven development at Bosch, development and testing of autonomous driving systems at Waymo, and testing of robotics software at XITASO. Researchers and practitioners can build on the contents of this paper to gain a fresh perspective on their activities and focus on the most pressing practices and challenges in developing robotics software today. 本文总结了机器人软件工程领域的行业最佳实践，涵盖NASA/Caltech、Bosch、Waymo和XITASO等机构的经验。 Robert Bocchino,Arne Nordmann,Allison Thackston,Andreas Angerer,Federico Ciccozzi,Ivano Malavolta,Andreas Wortmann cs.SE,cs.RO

authors_text: Minh-Long Luu,Zeyi Huang,Eric P. Xing,Yong Jae Lee,Haohan Wang
title: Expeditious Saliency-guided Mix-up through Random Gradient Thresholding
title_zh: 通过随机梯度阈值快速显着性引导混合
comment: Accepted Long paper at 2nd Practical-DL Workshop at AAAI 2023
comments: Accepted Long paper at 2nd Practical-DL Workshop at AAAI 2023
abstract: Mix-up training approaches have proven to be effective in improving the generalization ability of Deep Neural Networks. Over the years, the research community expands mix-up methods into two directions, with extensive efforts to improve saliency-guided procedures but minimal focus on the arbitrary path, leaving the randomization domain unexplored. In this paper, inspired by the superior qualities of each direction over one another, we introduce a novel method that lies at the junction of the two routes. By combining the best elements of randomness and saliency utilization, our method balances speed, simplicity, and accuracy. We name our method R-Mix following the concept of "Random Mix-up". We demonstrate its effectiveness in generalization, weakly supervised object localization, calibration, and robustness to adversarial attacks. Finally, in order to address the question of whether there exists a better decision protocol, we train a Reinforcement Learning agent that decides the mix-up policies based on the classifier's performance, reducing dependency on human-designed objectives and hyperparameter tuning. Extensive experiments further show that the agent is capable of performing at the cutting-edge level, laying the foundation for a fully automatic mix-up. Our code is released at [https://github.com/minhlong94/Random-Mixup].

Extensive experiments further show that the agent is capable of performing at the cutting-edge level, laying the foundation for a fully automatic mix-up. Our code is released at [https://github.com/minhlong94/Random-Mixup].
abstract_zh: 混合训练方法已被证明可以有效提高深度神经网络的泛化能力。多年来，研究界将混合方法扩展到两个方向，广泛努力改进显着性引导程序，但很少关注任意路径，从而导致随机化领域未被探索。在本文中，受到每个方向相对于另一个方向的优越品质的启发，我们介绍了一种位于两条路线交汇处的新颖方法。通过结合随机性和显着性利用的最佳元素，我们的方法平衡了速度、简单性和准确性。我们根据“随机混合”的概念将我们的方法命名为 R-Mix。我们证明了它在泛化、弱监督对象定位、校准和对抗性攻击的鲁棒性方面的有效性。最后，为了解决是否存在更好的决策协议的问题，我们训练了一个强化学习代理，该代理根据分类器的性能决定混合策略，减少对人为设计的目标和超参数调整的依赖。大量实验进一步表明，该代理能够达到尖端水平，为全自动混合奠定了基础。我们的代码发布在[https://github.com/minhlong94/Random-Mixup]。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合随机性和显著性引导的新型Mix-up方法R-Mix，通过随机梯度阈值技术提升深度神经网络的泛化能力与鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有研究过度关注显著性引导路径而忽视随机化路径的潜力，本文旨在融合两者优势以实现速度、简洁性与准确性的平衡。
AI.method: 设计R-Mix方法，结合显著性区域与随机梯度阈值生成混合样本，并引入强化学习代理自动决策Mix-up策略，减少人工调参依赖。
AI.result: 实验表明R-Mix在泛化、弱监督定位、校准及对抗鲁棒性方面优于现有方法，强化学习代理达到前沿水平并支持全自动Mix-up。
AI.conclusion: R-Mix通过融合随机性与显著性优势，为自动化的Mix-up训练提供了有效方案，强化学习代理的引入为未来研究奠定基础。

authors_text: Xinzhe Ni,Yong Liu,Hao Wen,Yatai Ji,Jing Xiao,Yujiu Yang
title: Multimodal Prototype-Enhanced Network for Few-Shot Action Recognition
title_zh: 用于少镜头动作识别的多模态原型增强网络
abstract: Current methods for few-shot action recognition mainly fall into the metric learning framework following ProtoNet, which demonstrates the importance of prototypes. Although they achieve relatively good performance, the effect of multimodal information is ignored, e.g. label texts. In this work, we propose a novel MultimOdal PRototype-ENhanced Network (MORN), which uses the semantic information of label texts as multimodal information to enhance prototypes. A CLIP visual encoder and a frozen CLIP text encoder are introduced to obtain features with good multimodal initialization. Then in the visual flow, visual prototypes are computed by a visual prototype-computed module. In the text flow, a semantic-enhanced (SE) module and an inflating operation are used to obtain text prototypes. The final multimodal prototypes are then computed by a multimodal prototype-enhanced (MPE) module. Besides, we define a PRototype SImilarity DiffErence (PRIDE) to evaluate the quality of prototypes, which is used to verify our improvement on the prototype level and effectiveness of MORN. We conduct extensive experiments on four popular few-shot action recognition datasets: HMDB51, UCF101, Kinetics and SSv2, and MORN achieves state-of-the-art results. When plugging PRIDE into the training stage, the performance can be further improved.
summary: Current methods for few-shot action recognition mainly fall into the metric learning framework following ProtoNet, which demonstrates the importance of prototypes. Although they achieve relatively good performance, the effect of multimodal information is ignored, e.g. label texts. In this work, we propose a novel MultimOdal PRototype-ENhanced Network (MORN), which uses the semantic information of label texts as multimodal information to enhance prototypes. A CLIP visual encoder and a frozen CLIP text encoder are introduced to obtain features with good multimodal initialization. Then in the visual flow, visual prototypes are computed by a visual prototype-computed module. In the text flow, a semantic-enhanced (SE) module and an inflating operation are used to obtain text prototypes. The final multimodal prototypes are then computed by a multimodal prototype-enhanced (MPE) module. Besides, we define a PRototype SImilarity DiffErence (PRIDE) to evaluate the quality of prototypes, which is used to verify our improvement on the prototype level and effectiveness of MORN. We conduct extensive experiments on four popular few-shot action recognition datasets: HMDB51, UCF101, Kinetics and SSv2, and MORN achieves state-of-the-art results. When plugging PRIDE into the training stage, the performance can be further improved.
abstract_zh: 目前的少镜头动作识别方法主要属于ProtoNet之后的度量学习框架，这说明了原型的重要性。尽管它们取得了相对较好的性能，但忽略了多模态信息的影响，例如标签文本。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种新颖的多模态原型增强网络（MORN），它使用标签文本的语义信息作为多模态信息来增强原型。引入 CLIP 视觉编码器和冻结 CLIP 文本编码器来获得具有良好多模态初始化的特征。然后在视觉流程中，视觉原型由视觉原型计算模块计算。在文本流中，使用语义增强（SE）模块和膨胀操作来获取文本原型。然后通过多模态原型增强 (MPE) 模块计算最终的多模态原型。此外，我们定义了一个原型相似度差异（PRIDE）来评估原型的质量，用于验证我们对原型水平和MORN有效性的改进。我们对四个流行的少镜头动作识别数据集：HMDB51、UCF101、Kinetics 和 SSv2 进行了广泛的实验，MORN 取得了最先进的结果。当将PRIDE插入训练阶段时，性能可以进一步提高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多模态原型增强网络（MORN），通过结合视觉和文本信息提升少样本动作识别的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有少样本动作识别方法（如ProtoNet）忽视了标签文本等多模态信息对原型的影响，限制了性能提升。
AI.method: 引入CLIP视觉编码器和冻结的文本编码器获取多模态特征，通过视觉原型计算模块、语义增强模块及多模态原型增强模块生成融合视觉与文本信息的原型，并设计PRIDE指标评估原型质量。
AI.result: 在HMDB51、UCF101、Kinetics和SSv2四个数据集上达到最先进水平，结合PRIDE训练进一步提升性能。
AI.conclusion: 多模态信息融合有效增强原型表征，MORN在少样本动作识别任务中表现出色，PRIDE验证了方法的有效性。

authors_text: Yannic Neuhaus,Maximilian Augustin,Valentyn Boreiko,Matthias Hein
title: Spurious Features Everywhere -- Large-Scale Detection of Harmful Spurious Features in ImageNet
title_zh: 无处不在的虚假特征——大规模检测 ImageNet 中的有害虚假特征
abstract: Benchmark performance of deep learning classifiers alone is not a reliable predictor for the performance of a deployed model. In particular, if the image classifier has picked up spurious features in the training data, its predictions can fail in unexpected ways. In this paper, we develop a framework that allows us to systematically identify spurious features in large datasets like ImageNet. It is based on our neural PCA components and their visualization. Previous work on spurious features often operates in toy settings or requires costly pixel-wise annotations. In contrast, we work with ImageNet and validate our results by showing that presence of the harmful spurious feature of a class alone is sufficient to trigger the prediction of that class. We introduce the novel dataset "Spurious ImageNet" which allows to measure the reliance of any ImageNet classifier on harmful spurious features. Moreover, we introduce SpuFix as a simple mitigation method to reduce the dependence of any ImageNet classifier on previously identified harmful spurious features without requiring additional labels or retraining of the model. We provide code and data at https://github.com/YanNeu/spurious_imagenet .
summary: Benchmark performance of deep learning classifiers alone is not a reliable predictor for the performance of a deployed model. In particular, if the image classifier has picked up spurious features in the training data, its predictions can fail in unexpected ways. In this paper, we develop a framework that allows us to systematically identify spurious features in large datasets like ImageNet. It is based on our neural PCA components and their visualization. Previous work on spurious features often operates in toy settings or requires costly pixel-wise annotations. In contrast, we work with ImageNet and validate our results by showing that presence of the harmful spurious feature of a class alone is sufficient to trigger the prediction of that class. We introduce the novel dataset "Spurious ImageNet" which allows to measure the reliance of any ImageNet classifier on harmful spurious features. Moreover, we introduce SpuFix as a simple mitigation method to reduce the dependence of any ImageNet classifier on previously identified harmful spurious features without requiring additional labels or retraining of the model. We provide code and data at https://github.com/YanNeu/spurious_imagenet .
abstract_zh: 仅深度学习分类器的基准性能并不能可靠地预测已部署模型的性能。特别是，如果图像分类器在训练数据中发现了虚假特征，则其预测可能会以意想不到的方式失败。在本文中，我们开发了一个框架，使我们能够系统地识别 ImageNet 等大型数据集中的虚假特征。它基于我们的神经 PCA 组件及其可视化。之前关于虚假特征的工作通常在玩具设置中进行，或者需要昂贵的像素级注释。 In contrast, we work with ImageNet and validate our results by showing that presence of the harmful spurious feature of a class alone is sufficient to trigger the prediction of that class.我们引入了新颖的数据集“Spurious ImageNet”，它可以测量任何 ImageNet 分类器对有害虚假特征的依赖程度。 Moreover, we introduce SpuFix as a simple mitigation method to reduce the dependence of any ImageNet classifier on previously identified harmful spurious features without requiring additional labels or retraining of the model.我们在 https://github.com/YanNeu/spurious_imagenet 提供代码和数据。

AI.motivation: 深度学习分类器的基准性能并不能准确预测部署模型的表现，因为模型可能在训练数据中学习到有害的虚假特征，导致预测失败。
AI.method: 基于神经PCA组件及其可视化，提出了一种系统识别虚假特征的框架，并引入Spurious ImageNet数据集和SpuFix方法，无需额外标签或重新训练即可减少对虚假特征的依赖。
AI.result: 成功识别了ImageNet中的有害虚假特征，并验证了这些特征的存在足以触发特定类别的预测。通过SpuFix方法有效减少了模型对这些特征的依赖。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法能够有效检测和缓解大型数据集中有害虚假特征的问题，为提升模型鲁棒性提供了新的思路和工具。

In particular, if the image classifier has picked up spurious features in the training data, its predictions can fail in unexpected ways. In this paper, we develop a framework that allows us to systematically identify spurious features in large datasets like ImageNet. It is based on our neural PCA components and their visualization. Moreover, we introduce SpuFix as a simple mitigation method to reduce the dependence of any ImageNet classifier on previously identified harmful spurious features without requiring additional labels or retraining of the model. authors_text: Kaixuan Lu,Xiao Huang
title: RCDT: Relational Remote Sensing Change Detection with Transformer
title_zh: RCDT：使用 Transformer 进行关系遥感变化检测
abstract: Deep learning based change detection methods have received wide attentoion, thanks to their strong capability in obtaining rich features from images. However, existing AI-based CD methods largely rely on three functionality-enhancing modules, i.e., semantic enhancement, attention mechanisms, and correspondence enhancement. The stacking of these modules leads to great model complexity. To unify these three modules into a simple pipeline, we introduce Relational Change Detection Transformer (RCDT), a novel and simple framework for remote sensing change detection tasks. The proposed RCDT consists of three major components, a weight-sharing Siamese Backbone to obtain bi-temporal features, a Relational Cross Attention Module (RCAM) that implements offset cross attention to obtain bi-temporal relation-aware features, and a Features Constrain Module (FCM) to achieve the final refined predictions with high-resolution constraints. Extensive experiments on four different publically available datasets suggest that our proposed RCDT exhibits superior change detection performance compared with other competing methods. The therotical, methodogical, and experimental knowledge of this study is expected to benefit future change detection efforts that involve the cross attention mechanism.
summary: Deep learning based change detection methods have received wide attentoion, thanks to their strong capability in obtaining rich features from images. However, existing AI-based CD methods largely rely on three functionality-enhancing modules, i.e., semantic enhancement, attention mechanisms, and correspondence enhancement. The stacking of these modules leads to great model complexity. To unify these three modules into a simple pipeline, we introduce Relational Change Detection Transformer (RCDT), a novel and simple framework for remote sensing change detection tasks. The proposed RCDT consists of three major components, a weight-sharing Siamese Backbone to obtain bi-temporal features, a Relational Cross Attention Module (RCAM) that implements offset cross attention to obtain bi-temporal relation-aware features, and a Features Constrain Module (FCM) to achieve the final refined predictions with high-resolution constraints. Extensive experiments on four different publically available datasets suggest that our proposed RCDT exhibits superior change detection performance compared with other competing methods. The therotical, methodogical, and experimental knowledge of this study is expected to benefit future change detection efforts that involve the cross attention mechanism.
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的变化检测方法因其从图像中获取丰富特征的强大能力而受到广泛关注。然而，现有的基于人工智能的CD方法很大程度上依赖于三个功能增强模块，即语义增强、注意力机制和对应增强。这些模块的堆叠导致模型非常复杂。为了将这三个模块统一到一个简单的管道中，我们引入了关系变化检测变压器（RCDT），这是一种用于遥感变化检测任务的新颖而简单的框架。所提出的RCDT由三个主要组件组成，一个用于获得双时态特征的权重共享Siamese Backbone，一个实现偏移交叉注意以获得双时态关系感知特征的关系交叉注意模块（RCAM），以及一个用于在高分辨率约束下实现最终精细预测的特征约束模块（FCM）。对四个不同的公开数据集的广泛实验表明，与其他竞争方法相比，我们提出的 RCDT 表现出卓越的变化检测性能。这项研究的理论、方法和实验知识预计将有益于未来涉及交叉注意机制的变化检测工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于Transformer的新型遥感变化检测框架RCDT，旨在简化现有方法的复杂性并提升检测性能。
AI.motivation: 现有基于AI的变化检测方法依赖多个功能模块（语义增强、注意力机制、对应增强），导致模型复杂度高，需统一为简单流程。
AI.method: RCDT包含三个组件：共享权重的Siamese Backbone提取双时相特征、Relational Cross Attention Module（RCAM）实现偏移交叉注意力、Features Constrain Module（FCM）进行高分辨率约束预测。
AI.result: 在四个公开数据集上的实验表明，RCDT相比其他方法在变化检测性能上表现更优。
AI.conclusion: RCDT通过统一模块设计和交叉注意力机制，为遥感变化检测提供了简单有效的解决方案，可为未来研究提供理论和方法支持。

authors_text: Sebastien Deschamps,Hichem Sahbi
title: Frugal Reinforcement-based Active Learning
title_zh: 基于节俭强化的主动学习
comment: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2203.11564
comments: arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2203.11564
abstract: Most of the existing learning models, particularly deep neural networks, are reliant on large datasets whose hand-labeling is expensive and time demanding. A current trend is to make the learning of these models frugal and less dependent on large collections of labeled data. Among the existing solutions, deep active learning is currently witnessing a major interest and its purpose is to train deep networks using as few labeled samples as possible. However, the success of active learning is highly dependent on how critical are these samples when training models. In this paper, we devise a novel active learning approach for label-efficient training. The proposed method is iterative and aims at minimizing a constrained objective function that mixes diversity, representativity and uncertainty criteria. The proposed approach is probabilistic and unifies all these criteria in a single objective function whose solution models the probability of relevance of samples (i.e., how critical) when learning a decision function. We also introduce a novel weighting mechanism based on reinforcement learning, which adaptively balances these criteria at each training iteration, using a particular stateless Q-learning model. Extensive experiments conducted on staple image classification data, including Object-DOTA, show the effectiveness of our proposed model w.r.t. several baselines including random, uncertainty and flat as well as other work.
summary: Most of the existing learning models, particularly deep neural networks, are reliant on large datasets whose hand-labeling is expensive and time demanding. A current trend is to make the learning of these models frugal and less dependent on large collections of labeled data. Among the existing solutions, deep active learning is currently witnessing a major interest and its purpose is to train deep networks using as few labeled samples as possible. However, the success of active learning is highly dependent on how critical are these samples when training models. In this paper, we devise a novel active learning approach for label-efficient training. The proposed method is iterative and aims at minimizing a constrained objective function that mixes diversity, representativity and uncertainty criteria. The proposed approach is probabilistic and unifies all these criteria in a single objective function whose solution models the probability of relevance of samples (i.e., how critical) when learning a decision function. We also introduce a novel weighting mechanism based on reinforcement learning, which adaptively balances these criteria at each training iteration, using a particular stateless Q-learning model. Extensive experiments conducted on staple image classification data, including Object-DOTA, show the effectiveness of our proposed model w.r.t. several baselines including random, uncertainty and flat as well as other work.
abstract_zh: 大多数现有的学习模型，特别是深度神经网络，都依赖于大型数据集，而这些数据集的手工标记成本高昂且耗时。当前的趋势是使这些模型的学习变得节俭并且减少对大量标记数据的依赖。在现有的解决方案中，深度主动学习目前引起了人们的广泛兴趣，其目的是使用尽可能少的标记样本来训练深度网络。然而，主动学习的成功很大程度上取决于这些样本在训练模型时的重要性。在本文中，我们设计了一种新颖的主动学习方法，用于标签高效的训练。所提出的方法是迭代的，旨在最小化混合多样性、代表性和不确定性标准的约束目标函数。所提出的方法是概率性的，并将所有这些标准统一在一个目标函数中，其解决方案对学习决策函数时样本的相关性概率（即关键程度）进行建模。我们还引入了一种基于强化学习的新颖加权机制，该机制使用特定的无状态 Q 学习模型在每次训练迭代中自适应地平衡这些标准。对主要图像分类数据（包括 Object-DOTA）进行的广泛实验表明了我们提出的模型的有效性。一些基线包括随机、不确定性和平坦以及其他工作。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于强化学习的高效主动学习方法，通过统一多样性、代表性和不确定性准则，减少深度学习对大规模标注数据的依赖。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习模型依赖大量标注数据，而手动标注成本高昂。传统主动学习方法难以有效平衡样本选择的多样性、代表性和不确定性，导致标注效率低下。
AI.method: 设计迭代优化方法，混合多样性、代表性和不确定性准则构建目标函数，并引入基于无状态Q学习的强化学习机制动态调整权重，建模样本相关性概率。
AI.result: 在Object-DOTA等图像分类数据集上，该方法优于随机选择、不确定性采样和扁平化等基线方法，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的强化学习加权主动学习框架能够自适应平衡多准则，提升标注效率，在减少数据依赖性方面具有显著优势。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于强化学习的高效主动学习方法，通过统一多样性、代表性和不确定性准则，减少深度学习对大规模标注数据的依赖。
ai_content.motivation: 现有深度学习模型依赖大量标注数据，而手动标注成本高昂。传统主动学习方法难以有效平衡样本选择的多样性、代表性和不确定性，导致标注效率低下。
ai_content.method: 设计迭代优化方法，混合多样性、代表性和不确定性准则构建目标函数，并引入基于无状态Q学习的强化学习机制动态调整权重，建模样本相关性概率。
ai_content.result: 在Object-DOTA等图像分类数据集上，该方法优于随机选择、不确定性采样和扁平化等基线方法，验证了其有效性。
ai_content.conclusion: 本文提出的强化学习加权主动学习框架能够自适应平衡多准则，提升标注效率，在减少数据依赖性方面具有显著优势。
